site: Corporate Name:  Aarish Group Investment, LLC dba Minit Saver 2
Corporate Name:  Aarish Group Investment, LLC dba Minit Saver 2

problem: Premium submersible turbine pump probe cable was defective causing no tank level reading when installed; regular and diesel probe cable connectors were severely corroded and stuck in the risers; corrosion buildup inside the risers caused the floats to bind.
solution: Replaced the premium probe cable, which restored normal operation. Lubricated and carefully removed the diesel and regular probe cable connectors using pliers, then scraped corrosion from the inside of the risers to allow the floats to move freely. All three probes now read correctly on the ATG display.
Technician removed the Open/Wear Parts filler lid and dropped it off to the dealer for service; installation was not performed by this technician.
parts: 1x OPW Aluminum Cover and Seal Re [1-21AC]

problem: Regular grade fuel probe was not working.
solution: Removed the probe and recalibrated it, then tested to verify operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 nozzle continued dispensing gasoline after the handle was released, and fueling point 3 fuel gallon price display did not reset to 0.000 for new transactions.
solution: Technician tested fueling point 1 and found the nozzle operating normally without issues. For fueling point 3, the display controller was stuck on error code E01; technician rebooted the commander and the system returned to normal operation.

problem: Customer reported that Extra Mile Reward loyalty program was not working at the dispenser.
solution: Technician tested the loyalty system with both cash and card payments, tested the Chevron app, and confirmed the site was properly registered in the app. Provided the customer with Extra Mile support phone number for any future issues.

problem: Register 2 pinpad was not accepting Apple Pay transactions.
solution: Inspected the Verifone MX915 pinpad and found the ViperPay software was outdated at version 4.07.08. Upgraded the software to version 4.07.11, initialized EMV on Register 2, and verified Apple Pay functionality with a successful test transaction.

problem: Pinpad software failed to load properly with an outdated version, and the nozzle boot flapper on pump 17 was extending too far forward.
solution: Reloaded the pinpad software to the current recommended version and replaced the nozzle boot. All functions tested normal after repairs.
Not stated
parts: 1x Meter C+ Encore Exch ; 2x Filter 800 HS-30

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were not dispensing fuel because the nozzle boot flappers were binding and preventing grade selection on all octane levels (87, 89, 93).
solution: The technician removed both nozzle boots and determined they could not be repaired, so they were replaced with expedited parts from Wayne. Both fueling positions 9 and 10 are now back in service.

problem: Fueling point 2 remains stuck in sale condition after prepay fuel transactions, requiring the cashier to manually cash out each sale before the dispenser can be used again.
solution: Technician determined that the stuck sale issue is caused by a faulty fueling point 2 SPM keypad and recommended replacing it to restore proper dispenser operation. The owner elected to postpone the repair at this time. Technician verified that the dispenser is already running the latest software versions (iGem version 85 and iXPay2 version 2.8.205.9) and confirmed with Shell Level 2 support that no additional software upgrades are needed for this configuration.

problem: Cash register number 2 printer was not working due to a faulty paper cutting blade.
solution: Replaced the printer and tested the transaction to verify proper operation.

problem: Dispenser nozzle assignments were configured incorrectly, and the ATG tank probe for the premium tank was not displaying inventory data.
solution: Corrected the nozzle assignments in the Commander, initialized fuel, sent generic prices to all dispensers, and verified each grade select button activated the correct motor. For the ATG issue, powered down the unit, removed the premium tank probe, installed a zip tie on the float stopper to secure its position, reinstalled the probe, and powered the ATG back on. Verified the premium tank is now reporting inventory data correctly.
Not stated

problem: Ruby CI payment terminal was down with suspected failed Satadom module, preventing fueling point operation.
solution: Technician power cycled and reseated the Satadom module, then replaced it when that failed. Reloaded the Commander using a 2-year-old backup from another site, reprogrammed and tested the system, verified point-of-sale configuration with Invenco and Mako networks, and confirmed all pump functions were normal.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 1, channel 3 dispenses more fuel than the prepaid amount allows, overrunning on premium and supreme grades.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 12 card reader was not reading or processing payment card transactions, displaying an error message to customers.
solution: Cycled power to the dispenser and allowed the system to fully boot and initialize, then retested the card reader to restore transaction processing capability.

problem: The POS system was unable to process payments or refunds for prepay transactions at the fueling points due to incorrect fueling department configuration with missing product codes.
solution: Troubleshot the POS and pump/dispenser connection issue and identified that the fueling department was incorrectly configured with missing product codes. Remote troubleshooting was attempted but the issue requires a complete software reload, which was not performed pending approval from the Circle K entity.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 7 card reader was not reading customer membership cards.
solution: Technician investigated and found no issue with the card readers; membership cards are working properly on fueling points 3 and 7.

problem: Fueling points 3, 4, 5, and 6 were in CIOPA error state, while the dispenser control receiver was only functioning on fueling points 1 and 2, resulting in an ongoing outage lasting nine months.
solution: The technician discovered missing pairing configuration for the in-dispenser subscriber units on fueling points 3-4 and 5-6. After power cycling the subscriber units and access point, then re-pairing all units, the dispenser control receiver returned to normal operation on all fueling points. Test transactions performed outdoors were successful.

problem: The Off Road Select dispenser had an exposed keypad that needed to be disabled.
solution: Replaced the off-road diesel panel to blank out the keypad so it is no longer visible or usable. This was completed as part of the scheduled project.

problem: Fueling point 8 dispenser display was stuck on 'RDM Starting' with a white screen, and the Veeder Root tank monitoring system showed multiple alarms including T1 leak alarm, T1 sudden loss alarm, and T3 high water alarm with 89 gallons of water detected in tank T3.
solution: The technician checked tank T1 with water detecting paste and found no water present; knocked the probe float and found it could move but may have had debris holding it up. The technician determined the tank leak alarm was triggered by an overnight test when the site kept the pump open. For the dispenser display issue, the technician wiped and reloaded the Red Board, identified that the USB 3.0 driver had failed, swapped it to USB 2.0, and reprogrammed the system; all functions tested normal after the repair.

problem: Fueling point 2 card reader was frozen, causing cards to get stuck inside.
solution: Power cycled the IXJade unit, which corrected the frozen condition and restored card reader functionality.

problem: Diesel fuel dispensers at fueling points 1, 2, 3, and 4 were running slow.
solution: Replaced both diesel filters on the affected dispensers. Found and reconnected the Texaco pump toppers that were unplugged in the electronic cabinet for fueling points 1 and 2, and 3 and 4. Adjusted the canopy time clock to run from 7 AM to 7 PM per store attendant request. All toppers and dispensers are now working correctly.

problem: The pinpad tap-to-pay function was not working on the dispenser.
solution: Technician performed a table download, which resolved the issue. All functions were tested with the customer and confirmed working.

problem: Fueling point 1 breakaway was damaged or non-functional.
solution: Replaced the breakaway on fueling point 1.

problem: Water was detected in the fuel tank sump.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 1 and 2 were adding 15 cents to the pump display when the nozzle was lifted, and customers could not redeem Extramile rewards on the dispensers.
solution: The technician verified that fueling point 4 and all dispensers are functioning correctly and that Extramile rewards are properly displayed on all DCR screens. The price jump issue on fueling points 1 and 2 was attributed to air in the product lines at the highest points of the system, which has since cleared out naturally.

problem: Dispenser fuel pumps were upgraded to Wayne equipment, requiring EMV file updates; additionally, fueling point 4 diesel pump had slow flow and was not counting gallons.
solution: Cycled power to the electronic display head and communication equipment to push updated EMV files to the dispensers, then downloaded and programmed the IXPay guide for the Gilbarco Passport system with Shell configuration. For fueling point 4, diagnosed a fragile Xflow meter as the cause of slow flow and no gallon counting; recommended meter replacement, but the site declined and requested cleaning instead, which was not performed due to technician inexperience with Xflow equipment.

problem: Forecourt controller (Commander 16) stuck in boot sequence with B1 status unable to establish communication with Phillips 76 payment network after system upgrade to Base 55; Ruby 2 register displaying black screen in A9 status; MX915 pinpad installation requested but unable to complete.
solution: Technician verified network settings (gateway, DNS, DHCP), confirmed site internet connectivity through Hughes Network diagnostics, replaced payment network interface cable, and adjusted screen layout per customer request. Issue remains unresolved at application/configuration level; site can dispense fuel but payment system functionality is limited pending further configuration, provisioning, or escalation to Phillips 76 network services.
parts: 1x 8043                       RDM RUBY2 POS Terminal ; 1x VERIFONE SATA DOM SITE CONTROL [ICM149-002-01-D] ; 1x VERIFONE Power Supply 100-240V [PWR050-001-01-B]

problem: Fueling point 1/2 diesel dispenser was running at slow flow rate.
solution: Technician replaced the filter multiple times and replaced the leak detector, but determined the submersible turbine pump motor was failing. Customer declined motor replacement, so the old leak detector was reinstalled. Dispenser now pumps at approximately 6–7 gallons per minute. Recommend customer replace the pump motor to restore normal flow rate.

problem: Fuel dispenser nozzle 5 or 6 requires multiple clicks before fuel begins to flow.
solution: Not stated

problem: The submersible turbine pump motor locked up because the fuel tank ran extremely low.
solution: Replaced the submersible turbine pump motor and tested all dispensers to confirm they are operating normally. Advised the dealer to maintain minimum fuel level above 15 inches to prevent recurrence of this issue.

problem: The DW-14 printer on fueling point 9 was not printing receipts.
solution: The technician reseated the cabling and power cycled the printer, but it did not resolve the issue, so the printer was replaced under warranty. The technician also power cycled fueling point 5, which restored the money/gallons display and corrected the off-centered pump/price unit display.

problem: Fueling points 5 and 6 were stopping randomly mid-sale.
solution: The technician found that the pulsar wire was not fully seated into the pulsar connector, which was causing the sporadic shutdowns. The wire was reseated and both fueling points were tested and confirmed to be working correctly at 100%; no parts replacement was needed.

problem: The receipt printer at fueling point 10 will not print receipts and is displaying an error indicator light.
solution: Technician disconnected and reseated both power and data cables but the error condition persisted. The DW-12 thermal printer assembly has failed and requires replacement; a return visit is scheduled to install the replacement printer (part number 890477-R01/WU005878-0001) and restore receipt printing functionality.

problem: The keypad on pump #8 dispenser was unavailable.
solution: Replaced the USB cable. This is not a stocked inventory item.

problem: The dispenser register display was not functioning properly.
solution: Installed a backup display unit and tested fueling point 1 to verify normal operation. The technician informed the site of the 1-year part warranty coverage and waived the trip charge.

problem: Fueling points 1, 3, 5, 6, and 8 had breached submersible turbine pump (STP) modules.
solution: Replaced the breached STP modules for fueling points 8, 6, 5, 3, and 1.

problem: Multiple fueling points on the Ovation2 Anthem dispenser were not functioning properly: fueling point 5 was not allowing customers to select fuel grade, fueling points 1, 12, 8, and 10 were showing COIPA errors, and most dispensers were displaying 'Pay Inside Only' messages.
solution: Technician rebooted the dispenser from the DX monitor to restore normal operation. A return trip was noted as potentially necessary to address the wireless communication issue and ensure all fueling points were fully functional.

problem: Fueling points 5 and 6 were reported as having slow flow.
solution: Technician arrived on site and observed both fueling points operating at full flow with all fuel grades dispensing normally. Confirmed that filters had been recently replaced and the unit was running faster following the replacement.

problem: Fueling points 1, 2, and 6 were running slowly and stuck on a 'moment please' display message.
solution: Rebooted all units and reset the passport register and EDH (electronic display head) inside. The site did not have the correct password, which was resolved during the service call. Advised the customer that if the issue returns, a technician will need to return with an iButton to reimage the EDH.

problem: Fueling point #5 display screen was blank and unresponsive.
solution: Not stated

problem: Dispenser showing error on register with all pumps set to pay inside only; fueling point 1 backup display was not functioning properly.
solution: Technician restored power to the access point that had been turned off, repositioned cables on the Cybera unit to correct ports to bring the unit back online, and reconnected the fueling point 1 controller. Determined that fueling point 1 requires a new backup display to fully resolve the issue.
Not stated
Not stated
parts: 1x 8906                        RDM CLGB Current Loop (RS-232) ; 3x WAYNE MODULE, DFS WIRELESS IN- [WU019855-0001]

problem: Site was not ready for installation with missing modem for online payment processing and incorrectly routed ethernet cables; dispensers already had templates loaded that conflicted with planned installation.
solution: Technician installed and programmed equipment as far as possible given site constraints, cleaned wires, and set up in available space. Recommendation: Site should provide modem and correct ethernet cable routing before future visits, and communicate back-office requirements in advance to avoid reloading dispenser templates.

Technician installed in-dispenser wired communication units (DFS Avalan) on fueling points 1 through 8, fabricated and terminated Ethernet cables for each dispenser, routed cables properly, and programmed the iGem units using the Wayne remote. Note: Site was not ready for full installation due to water in tanks and ongoing interior wiring work; default passwords were not changed.

problem: Fueling point 4 mid-grade fuel was not dispensing, and fueling points 3, 4, and 9 were showing COIPA (communication) errors.
solution: The technician replaced one SU (selection unit) on fueling point 9, reloaded and reprogrammed the iGem on fueling points 3 and 4, and tested mid-grade and non-ethanol fuel dispensing on fueling point 4 to verify the repair. No COIPA errors remained after the work.

problem: Dispenser processor configuration needed verification and update to FISERV system during initial installation.
solution: Technician verified the configuration on-site, reloaded the commander to the latest base 55 software, and confirmed the system was operating on FISERV. All systems tested normal after the reload.

problem: Fuel price display remote was not programmed during dispenser setup.
solution: Programmed the Fuel Price Display remote. Identified missing equipment that has not yet been installed and located it in original packaging but did not install it at this time.
Not stated

problem: The register was frozen on the commander display at screen A8 due to full memory, causing the site to be down.
solution: The technician power cycled the unit, removed the dispenser bottom, reseated internal cables to the memory units, and reloaded the base version of the system through the commander. The register came back up normally and the unit returned to screen A9.

problem: All dispensers were delivering slow fuel flow rates of 7-12 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced the regular filters on all fueling points and rebooted fueling point 7 which had a stuck prompt. After purging the system, flow rate improved to 8-9 gallons per minute.

problem: Dispensers 1 through 8 were all out of calibration, with each one over-dispensing product by 6 to 8 gallons per transaction.
solution: Technician recalibrated all 8 dispensers back into state tolerance. Results should be emailed to the customer as requested.

problem: Fueling point 7 nozzle boot flapper was moving too far forward and not engaging correctly, causing transactions to not clear properly at the register and resulting in short money after prepay on pump 6.
solution: Replaced the nozzle boot on fueling point 7 with a new one and tested the fueling point to confirm proper operation. The fueling point is now back in service.

problem: Fueling point 4 card reader was not accepting cards for payment.
solution: Technician replaced the card reader assembly after determining that either a foreign object was lodged inside or the EMV chip reader clip was stuck in the closed position. Multiple test transactions confirmed the unit is now operating properly.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were displaying E01 error and were offline, unable to process transactions.
solution: Technician verified network connections and rebooted the equipment without success, then identified the DFS wired in-dispenser Ethernet switch as faulty by swapping it with another unit and confirming the error followed the switch. Replaced the faulty switch with part number WU021906-0001, rebooted the equipment, and confirmed both fueling points were restored to online operation with successful test transactions.

problem: All regular and premium fuel dispensers were not dispensing and rewards were not working, with a magvfc error on the display.
solution: Technician investigated and found the fuel tank had been depleted to 285 gallons (6 inches on the tank gauge) and a fuel delivery had been made the same day. Testing revealed no slow flow issues or locked rotor on the guardian box, and all dispensers were operating normally at 100% capacity.

problem: Fueling point 10 card reader was not reading any cards, taking the fueling point out of service.
solution: Technician replaced the faulty card reader with a new one and verified it was working by successfully testing chip transactions. The old card reader was determined to be under warranty coverage.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: Fueling point 11 dispenser display screen was frozen and unresponsive, with no transactions recorded since the previous night.
solution: The technician power cycled and rebooted the dispenser. After the reboot, the display screen returned to normal operation and transactions began processing correctly through credit and debit card acceptance.

problem: Fueling points 5 and 6 were dispensing regular and premium fuel slowly at 5 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician replaced the filters and purged the fuel lines. The flow rate was monitored and confirmed to be restored to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 6 was frozen displaying 'IXpay starting' on the display screen and out of service despite a reboot attempt.
solution: Technician diagnosed a broken SCD (secure card device), replaced it with a new unit, and loaded the software. Transactions were tested successfully to confirm the repair.

problem: Fueling point 12 diesel nozzle automatic shut-off was not engaging, causing spillage.
solution: Replaced the nozzle, purged the fuel line, and tested the fueling point. Monitored several transactions to confirm the automatic shut-off was functioning properly.

problem: Fueling point 3 was reported to be rebooting itself and out of service; fueling point 7 had a loose unleaded grade selection button, missing diesel sticker, and was printing two receipts per transaction.
solution: Technician arrived on-site and found fueling point 3 actively in service and operating normally. Technician monitored dispenser operation but was unable to reproduce the reported issues; root cause and resolution not stated in notes.
parts: 1x WAYNE Decal, Octane Kit (85 Th ; 2x WAYNE Nozzle Boot Plastic PTS

problem: Fueling point #6 had a faulty jade board, and fueling point #5 had a breached submersible turbine pump.
solution: Replaced the jade board on fueling point #6 with the customer-supplied part, reset and reprogrammed it, then tested with a debit card after EMV initialization. Replaced the breached submersible turbine pump on fueling point #5, injected it, and tested with a debit card. Note: The customer-supplied jade board is not under warranty.

problem: Fueling points 3, 9, and 11 had non-communicating dispensers (DCR units), with both C-18 units displaying error messages and failing to initialize.
solution: Technician moved cabling around on fueling points 9 and 10 ANTX board to resolve the Anthem touch screen unresponsiveness on fueling point 9. Michael initialized the DCR units, added fuel departments, registered equipment, and paired devices. Additionally, one non-functional canopy Texaco sign was found to be missing a driver; imaging will need to return with the replacement driver to complete the repair.

problem: Pro Gauge tank monitoring system installation required at fueling point.
solution: Installed new Pro Gauge Ultimate tank monitor with printer, two 101 DMP probes, one 69 DMP probe, and two large sump sensors. Programmed the tank monitor, calibrated all probes, and tested the system. Trained customer on operation and printing functions.

problem: The old Integra 100 tank gauge system needed to be replaced.
solution: Removed the old Integra 100 gauge and installed a new Pro Gauge LX Ultimate system. The existing 924B probes and printer were reused, the system was programmed, tank levels were checked and adjusted, and PDS Solutions was verified to have visibility of all tanks.

problem: Fueling points 1, 2, 7, and 8 card readers were declining all credit and debit cards.
solution: Technician found that fueling points 1 and 2 were operating normally. For fueling points 7 and 8, the technician power-cycled the equipment and rebooted the system, after which both fueling points resumed accepting cards properly. Transactions were verified as working.

problem: Fueling point 8 keypad was not functioning, preventing customer transactions.
solution: Technician corrected the keypad programming and wiring, then successfully tested transactions to confirm operation. Receipt printer errors on fueling points 4 and 6 were also resolved, and the cash drawer was installed and verified working.

problem: The nozzle handle spring on the number one gasoline dispenser was broken and flying off.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle boot and tested the dispenser, restoring it to full operating condition.

problem: The fueling point 6 nozzle was broken and not dispensing fuel.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle.

problem: Fueling point 11 diesel dispensers were pumping fuel slowly due to a bad nozzle and dirty filter.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle and filter, then tested and purged the fuel line to restore proper flow.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" Leaded,11B, G ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Premium probe was showing zero and not detecting proper fluid levels.
solution: Cleaned and adjusted the premium probe floats and magnets which had debris buildup preventing proper operation. Also identified that the sump sensors were failing intermittently and entering alarm mode; customer needs to replace 5 large sump sensors and 1 small sump sensor. After cleaning the premium probe and resetting the tank monitor system and customer reports file, all tank probes were verified to be reading properly.

problem: The dispenser and fueling point had no power, and the premium dispenser breakaway was broken.
solution: The technician found that the emergency stop had been activated and reset the shunt trip breaker to restore power to the fuel master and pumps. He replaced the broken breakaway on the premium dispenser and tested all systems to verify full functionality.

problem: Fueling point 3 was offline from the network due to continuous rebooting of the Jade board.
solution: Technician performed an iXReset and debug procedure on the Jade board, reloaded the latest IDPOS software version onto both the Jade board and SCD (submersible card device), reconfigured the Jade board, and rebooted the unit. Fueling point 3 was tested successfully. Additionally, technician power-cycled the printer on fueling point 11 to resolve a printing issue with a red error light, and the printer returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 6 had a leak at the breakaway connector.
solution: The technician removed the old breakaway and installed a new one, then tested the fueling point to confirm it was pumping at full flow and operating normally.

problem: A customer drove away from fueling point 8 with the hose still attached to her vehicle, pulling the hose and nozzle away from the pump.
solution: The technician located the hose and nozzle from store staff, inspected them for damage (found only minor scrapes), reinstalled the breakaway connector, tightened all connections, and purged air from the hose while testing for leaks. No leaks were found during operation.

problem: Fueling point 9 receipt printer was reported as not feeding paper.
solution: Technician checked dispenser diagnostics and confirmed printer status was OK, then performed a test transaction which successfully printed a receipt. No issues were found; the printer was operating properly.

problem: Fueling point 3 Premium dispenser displaying code 5047; fueling point 3 regular unleaded meter rotor unit spinning continuously and requires replacement; leak detector motor was non-functional.
solution: Replaced leak detector motor and capacitor and tested successfully. Power cycled fueling point 3 Premium dispenser and bled nozzle to clear code 5047. Fueling point 3 regular unleaded meter rotor unit requires replacement.
parts: 1x Leak Detector (GAS) ; 1x Capacitor 25 MFD ; 1x Motor 1.5 HP

problem: Fueling point 1 dispenser display was continuously rebooting and showing an error, with the fuel pump not responding.
solution: Performed a debug reset on the Jade board, loaded the latest IDPOS software onto the Jade board and SCD, reconfigured the Jade board, and allowed the dispenser to fully initialize. Also replaced the nozzle because the auto-shutoff spring was missing, which was causing fuel to spill when the nozzle was removed from the boot; tested the replacement nozzle successfully.

problem: Fuel dispensers 1, 2, 3, and 4 were running outdated IXPay software and unable to communicate with the network due to a disconnected Cybera WAN cable at the switch/router.
solution: Technician reconnected the unplugged Cybera WAN cable at the unmanned switches and routers, confirmed primary network connection with Cybera support, reset the manager password, upgraded dispensers 1/2 and 3/4 to the latest IXPay software, upgraded the Commander and registers to Base 55 version, reloaded the backup, contacted Verifone to send patch software to the Fuel Controller, and verified all functions normal after testing with Exxon.

problem: Diesel pump on fueling point 7/8 was dispensing slowly, especially when both sides were in use, due to rust and debris contamination in the submersible turbine pump check valve assembly causing a sealing issue.
solution: Technician removed and inspected the submersible turbine pump motor check valve assembly, cleaned rust and debris from the housing, reinstalled the check valve, and installed a new diesel filter. Flow rate improved from 6 GPM to approximately 8 GPM, and system seating pressure remained above 10 PSI.

problem: Fueling point 3 card reader was out of service with a damaged screen that would not read cards.
solution: Cleaned the QR code area on the screen after finding a defective screen protector was blocking the scanner. The card reader then functioned properly.

problem: Fueling point 6 dispenser was not working and displaying an 'Out of Service' message.
solution: Technician found the keypad in a breached state, replaced it, injected the debit key encryption, and reloaded the board to install the latest firmware update. After full reboot and testing, the fueling point returned to normal operation.

problem: Three fueling points were out of service: FP3 and FP4 had slow flow on all fuel grades, FP6 card reader was slow and display was down, and FP8 was shutting off during fueling.
solution: Technician replaced both regular and premium filters on FP3 and FP4 to restore normal product flow. Rebooted the displays on FP6 and FP8 to resolve the card reader and shutdown issues.

problem: Dispenser pump #1 is not working and the Tank Sentinel ATG probe is not displaying a reading.
solution: Technician determined that the ATG system is experiencing a memory error and that replacement parts are no longer available for this model. Technician recommended that the dealer order a new EVO ATG system as a replacement. Dealer opted to cancel the service call pending further decision.

problem: Dispenser printer was not printing receipts completely and could not cut receipts properly; fueling points 2 and 4 displays showed KSB errors indicating the SPM keypads were breached.
solution: Replaced the faulty RP330 printer with a new unit and tested successfully. Replaced both compromised SPM keypads on fueling points 2 and 4, injected debit key encryption, and restored the dispensers to operational mode with successful test transactions performed on both units.

problem: Premium fuel dispenser was making a clicking sound and would not dispense fuel when customers attempted to pump.
solution: Technician inspected the hanging hardware and found the breakaway connector was not fully connected, likely from a previous drive-off incident. The breakaway was reconnected and the dispenser was retested successfully and returned to service.

problem: All fuel dispensers were reported running slow with flow rates below 5 GPM on all grades, particularly at fueling points 1-4.
solution: The technician inspected all hydraulic lines and motors for leaks, tested filter pressures on regular unleaded and premium lines (all within normal range of 30-40 PSI), and verified dispenser flow rates were actually normal at approximately 8 GPM, indicating no active pump issue. The technician also replaced the nozzle at fueling point 6 due to a missing auto shut-off spring.

problem: Fueling point 5 was offline from the network.
solution: The technician disabled F56, cycled power on the jade board, and re-enabled F56, which restored fueling point 5 to online status. The technician also cycled power on the fueling point 12 printer to restore it to online status, tested all dispensers, and confirmed operation with the site contact.

problem: Premium dispenser was not dispensing fuel despite the meter valve opening during test transactions.
solution: Technician inspected the hanging hardware and found the breakaway connector was not fully connected, possibly from a previous drive-off incident. Reconnected the breakaway, retested the dispenser successfully, and returned it to console mode for normal operation.

problem: Dispensers 3 and 4 had incorrect addresses configured; dispenser 3 was incorrectly set to address 4.
solution: Technician reconfigured the addresses to the correct settings for both dispensers 3 and 4, then tested all functions and confirmed 100% operation.

Installation of two dual-hose C18 fuel dispensers with Anthem B23/4 four-hose assemblies (two diesel and two regular 90) was completed in phases due to site constraints and contractor activities. Technician set up equipment, connected cabling, performed step-up upgrade on ANTX board, updated SPM keypad software, configured Managed Module, initialized fuel system, verified all dispensers operational for prepay transactions, and labeled fueling points. Return visit required to install DX Monitor and DX Promote on media box and to confirm Shell EPS network activation.

problem: Diesel dispensers 1 and 2 had dirty filters causing reduced flow.
solution: Replaced the diesel filters on both dispensers and tested flow rate to confirm 8 to 10 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling point 10 on the Ovation2 dispenser was not dispensing fuel despite being rebooted.
solution: The technician diagnosed the issue by checking iGem programming and Antx template (both good), testing in standalone mode, and removing the hose and nozzle. The solenoid valve on the REC 90 was found to be faulty and was replaced. After replacement, the technician tested the dispenser and confirmed full flow dispensing; fueling point 10 is now back in service.

problem: Submersible turbine pump sump lids and gasket were damaged on the regular fuel, diesel, and premium fueling points, and three latch covers were broken.
solution: Replaced three latch covers (CLC-33), replaced two regular fuel sump lids, and replaced one diesel sump lid.

problem: Regular grade fuel dispenser was flowing slowly while other grades were operating normally.
solution: Technician performed a rolling shutdown of the dispenser, replaced the regular grade filters, and purged the system. After completion, all functions tested normal.

problem: Pinpad 2 was offline, causing register 2 to go down and creating register software synchronization issues.
solution: Technician logged into the C-Store configuration client and found the pinpad 2 secure port had been incorrectly changed from port 5000 to port 500. The port was corrected back to 5000, the pinpad was reset, and the system came back online with all functions tested and working.

problem: Fueling point 7 and 8 display screen was blank.
solution: Replaced and programmed the jade board on fueling point 7, then tested both fueling points 7 and 8 to confirm they were working properly.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: Site required a rebrand from current branding to Amoco, including software upgrades to dispensers and registers; during the upgrade process, fueling points 1, 4, and 6 displayed security module errors, and a fuel leak was discovered in the Rec-90 filter on fueling point 5/6.
solution: Successfully upgraded fueling points 2, 3, and 5 to the latest iX Pay software and reinjected the security module encryption key; replaced the leaking Rec-90 filter on fueling point 5/6 after shutting off the shear valve; upgraded both Ruby2 registers and Commander CI to the latest Amoco Base 55 version with payment network and loyalty configuration; upgraded both pin pads with debit encryption and completed EMV/PDL file downloads; tested all upgraded fueling points and payment methods successfully. The store owner declined to replace the security modules on fueling points 1, 4, and 6, and the technician recommended removing 5–10 inches of contaminated fuel from the dispenser sump.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70120] ; 2x VERIFONE Key Inject Field load [999-DEP-00171]

solution: Not stated

problem: Right-side and left-side submersible turbine pump leak detectors and other components are corroded; vault sumps contain approximately one-quarter liquid each due to water accumulation.
solution: Technician opened all sump basin and submersible turbine pump supply access points and extracted all water from each sump basin. Recommend regular sump basin inspection and maintenance to prevent future water accumulation and corrosion.

problem: The ATG tank probe system locked up and displayed incorrect readings due to a power glitch.
solution: Shut down the system for 5 minutes, then powered it back on. All readings returned to normal and the system was functioning correctly. No further action required.

problem: Fueling point 7 diesel hose was leaking at the connection where the nozzle handle attaches.
solution: Replaced the diesel nozzle and swivel assembly on the fueling point, tested full flow, and returned the fueling point to service.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" Leaded,11B, G ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x

problem: Fueling point 11 hose assembly broke away from the dispenser, causing a gasoline spill and disconnecting the breakaway from the nozzle.
solution: The technician cleaned up the gasoline spill, cleaned the breakaway assembly with gasoline gel, and reconnected the breakaway to the nozzle and hose. The technician then tested the transaction and fuel flow to confirm normal operation.

problem: The 90-degree elbow fill adapter at the regular unleaded fueling point was loose and leaking during fuel delivery.
solution: Removed the fill adapter, applied pipe sealant to the threads, and securely reinstalled it using a pipe wrench. No leaks were observed after the repair.

problem: Fueling point 11 hose disconnected from breakaway due to drive-off incident causing dispenser to go out of service, and fueling point 6 display frozen on advertisement screen with dispenser unable to reboot.
solution: Reconnected the breakaway hose on fueling point 11, replaced the breached keypad that was damaged by vibration from the drive-off incident, and restored the dispenser to operational status with successful test transaction. For fueling point 6, performed an iX Reset on the Jade board and reloaded the IDPOS and SCD software, then reconfigured the Jade board to bring the dispenser back online with successful testing.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 1 had no flow on all grades and fueling point 5 diesel was stopping mid-sale.
solution: For fueling point 1, performed test transactions and found regular unleaded and premium dispensing properly at 8 gallons per minute with no interruptions; checked diagnostics and found no error codes, so no issue could be reproduced on site. For fueling point 5, found a slow leak from the breakaway caused by a damaged blue O-ring/gasket from a previous drive-off incident; replaced the breakaway and tested the diesel successfully with no leaks or mid-sale stoppages.

problem: Fueling point 5 diesel dispenser had a slow leak from the breakaway coupling caused by a damaged blue O-ring/gasket from a previous drive-off incident.
solution: Replaced the breakaway coupling with a new unit, secured all hanging hardware, and tested the diesel grade to confirm no leaks or mid-sale stoppages.

problem: Two card readers and one display screen were not functioning properly.
solution: Replaced both EMV card readers and the 10.4-inch display screen. During testing after replacement, a third defective card reader was discovered and also replaced.
parts: 1x 10.4 DISPLAY E700 NON KIT ; 2x Card Reader/Card Reader [UX300]

problem: Fueling point 3 has a failed flow control board and is offline on the point-of-sale system.
solution: Replaced the flow control board on fueling point 3. Fueling point 5 was bagged offline, tested by pumping $10 through it, and confirmed to be functioning correctly with no leaks or hardware issues.

problem: Two sensors were constantly triggering alarms: the fueling points 5 and 6 sensor and the premium submersible turbine pump sensor, and the submersible turbine pump sumps were full of water.
solution: Replaced both faulty sensors. The technician noted that the sumps need to be drained of water and recommended scheduling a separate service call to remove all water from the submersible turbine pump sumps.

problem: Two issues reported: fueling points 10, 11, and 12 had card reader printer doors not shutting properly, and fueling points 4 and 8 had pinpads that looked different from other pumps with extra buttons, raising concerns about possible tampering.
solution: Technician replaced the printer door latches on fueling points 10, 11, and 12 using Walmart-provided parts and verified all locks functioned properly and latched securely. Technician investigated fueling points 4 and 8 and found no faults at that time.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were displaying a network offline message and were not operational.
solution: The technician checked the network connection at the subscriber unit mounted inside the dispenser and inspected the wiring. Recommend attaching diagnostic photos, video, and logs, then creating a network escalation ticket on the DFS extranet if the issue persists.

problem: Fueling point 2 card reader has no power and is not working, and the regular grade product cable needs replacement.
solution: Technician determined the card reader requires replacement but customer decided to consider the repair. Technician noted that a replacement product cable is needed and will return with the cable on a follow-up visit.

problem: Fueling point 7 experienced a drive-off incident; the nozzle and hose were left on the ground after equipment recovery.
solution: Technician reinstalled the breakaway coupling and inspected the nozzle and hose for damage. Finding no damage or leaks, the equipment was returned to service and tested successfully by the customer during fueling with no leaks observed. No parts were replaced.

problem: Customers' cards were getting stuck in the card readers at fueling points 3 and 7, requiring the pump to be powered off and reset to retrieve them.
solution: The technician tested the card readers multiple times at fueling points 3 and 7 with the customer present and found no problem. The technician also fixed the printer door locks on fueling points 10 and 11.

problem: Fueling point 7 unihose hold-open latch was not disengaging, taking the pump out of service.
solution: Technician found the latch spring was missing and replaced the nozzle. After purging the system and confirming no leaks, the technician rebooted the fueling point when the IDPOS failed, and the unit returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 3 printer was jammed, and fueling points 1 and 2 were not working due to a communication port error on the IXJade controller.
solution: Cleared the jam in the fueling point 3 printer. Replaced the communication cable in the dispenser with a spare ethernet cable to resolve the communication port error, bringing fueling points 1 and 2 back online.

problem: Integra 100 dispenser was displaying a memory full error stating there is no more space left on LTNV storage.
solution: Technician cycled power to the dispenser and reseated the SD and USB chips. After the system restarted, all systems returned to normal operation. Technician noted that the customer expects the issue to recur and provided quotes for chip replacement and for replacing the dispenser with a Propane unit.

problem: Fueling point 5 had a flapper issue, and the register needed software setup and pairing.
solution: Loaded software and paired the C18 to the commander to get the register operational. Replaced the nozzle boot on fueling point 5 to fix the flapper issue. Adjusted a past transaction by adding the remaining $5.

problem: Water was pooling in the submersible turbine pump sump below the boot seals.
solution: Technician removed 2 gallons of water from the sump. Verify that pump seals are intact and sump drain is clear to prevent future water accumulation.
Not stated
parts: 2x MOHAMED ELSAYED ELTHAWEY ; 1x WAYNE Solenoid Valve Proportio ; 1x WYN iGEM CPU Board ; 1x WAYNE Check Valve and Relief, ; 1x WAYNE Program Power Distributi

problem: The submersible turbine pump motor was displaying an error code because the guardian controller for the diesel system was stuck on extended run mode.
solution: The technician reset the guardian controller and tested all systems, which restored operation to 100%. The issue was likely caused by low fuel level triggering the low fuel alarm; the technician recommends monitoring fuel levels to prevent recurrence.

problem: Fueling point 1/2 had a stuck card reader and fueling point 3/4 displayed an E01 error.
solution: Technician found the in-dispenser wireless unit had lost communication; replaced the unit and resynced it after locating the missing key for fueling point 3/4, restoring full function. For fueling point 1/2, no physical issues were found, so technician resent the EMV table files to clear any potential corruption in the dispenser tables.

problem: Fueling point 6 was closed and displaying a business hours message.
solution: Technician replaced the jade board and confirmed the new jade board MAC address. Transactions were tested successfully to verify the repair.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Fueling point 8 card reader slot was not engaging with cards and was not responsive; the site attempted reset and reboot without success.
solution: Technician performed a power cycle on the pump. After power cycle, transactions were tested successfully using chip card and contactless payment methods.

problem: Fueling points 3, 4, 9, and 10 were dispensing premium gasoline at very slow flow rates.
solution: Removed the two filters from the premium fuel lines on those fueling points and installed two new filters with installation dates marked on them. Verified that premium fuel flow was restored to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 2 was declining credit and debit cards intermittently.
solution: The technician power-cycled and rebooted the dispenser, which restored card acceptance. Both credit and debit cards were then accepted properly on fueling point 2.

problem: Fueling point 5 Unihose retractor cable was reported as broken and preventing the hose from retracting, putting the pump out of service.
solution: Technician inspected the Unihose retractor cable and found it to be in good condition. The pump was restored to service.

problem: Fueling point 10 universal hose was disconnected at the breakaway coupling and out of service.
solution: The technician reconnected the breakaway coupling and tested the fueling point. Fueling point 10 was verified to be back in service and pumping at full flow.

problem: Fueling point 6 was not allowing grade selection, though all other functions worked normally and a reset did not resolve the issue.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle boot with a new one. Grade selection is now working properly on fueling point 6.

problem: Fueling point 6 card reader unable to read cards, including both chip and magnetic strip, despite cleaning attempts and green indicator light.
solution: Technician diagnosed a hardware failure in the card reader through diagnostics testing and replaced the card reader unit. After replacement, the fueling point processed cards successfully in testing.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: Fueling point 3 uni-hose flapper was not registering when the nozzle was removed, preventing customers from selecting fuel grades.
solution: The technician removed the nozzle boot from the dispenser and found the spring that holds the flap was not working. The nozzle boot was replaced, and the flapper now registers properly.

problem: All four fueling points went offline and the register showed a red X, preventing customers from prepaying for fuel.
solution: The technician determined the LAN card on the Commander was malfunctioning by observing the A1-2 transmit light flashing without receive activity. The technician moved the communication cable from A1-5 to A2-5, reconfigured the Fuel Driver, and rebooted the system, which restored all fueling points to online status. All stuck sales were cleared and the site was able to process prepay transactions again.

Completed media onboarding, replaced breached submersible turbine pump at fueling point 6, repaired cables on register and commander, and verified debit and credit transactions working correctly both inside and outside.

problem: Diesel flow rate appeared slow to the customer, and the media reader was not working.
solution: Technician tested the media reader and found it non-functional, then contacted DX Global who identified a configuration issue on their end and took responsibility for the fix. Diesel flow rate testing showed over 10 GPM, which is normal for a non-high-speed meter, so no issue was found with the fuel system.

Technician reloaded the submersible turbine pump software on dispensers 3 through 6, as dispensers 3 and 4 were attempting to load outdated software. All pumps returned to normal operation. Technician recommended that the site consider removing the Edge device (model T93) to prevent automatic reboots at 8 PM, and noted that the software is outdated.

problem: Diesel and unleaded fuel filters needed replacement on multiple fueling points.
solution: Technician replaced the diesel filter on fueling points 15 and 16, and replaced unleaded filters on fueling points 1, 2, 3, 4, 15, and 16.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 3x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Fueling point 7 had a breached sealing interface module (SPM).
solution: Not stated

problem: Point of sale register screen was flickering.
solution: Replaced the defective C18 register with a new M182-001-01-NAA unit (serial 660-240-582). The original register (serial 660-240-583) will be sent to Verifone for warranty repair.

problem: Site required a complete rebranding from Amoco to BP, including software upgrade, new networking equipment installation, pinpad injection with updated payment software, and dispenser upgrades; additionally, fueling points 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 8 were found to have breached SPM keypads.
solution: Technician performed end-of-day/month/year reporting, backed up existing Verifone Commander configuration, replaced Cybera 325 with new Mako router and 24-port switch, loaded BP Buypass software 55.02.03, injected MX915 pinpads with Amoco ViperPay software with updated debit encryption, programmed BP Earnify Loyalty and MobilePay, established BP/Amoco network communication with successful test transaction, and inspected Wayne Ovation2 dispensers. Breached SPM keypads on fueling points 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 8 require replacement.

problem: Fueling point 5 was not dispensing fuel because the dispenser control relay was locked up after a customer pulled the credit card out too soon, causing a point-of-sale error.
solution: The technician initialized the dispenser control relay for fueling point 5 and power cycled the junction board. All functions tested back to 100% operation.

problem: Indoor payment system was not functioning on the dispenser.
solution: Technician cycled power to the dispenser, Commander, Edge Pro, and Cybera box. All systems were tested and confirmed to be running properly.

problem: Fueling point 2 had a meter issue caused by a worn and ripped gasket in the Minnesota check valve.
solution: The technician removed and inspected the solenoid valve and Minnesota valve, finding the solenoid valve in good condition but the Minnesota valve gasket worn out and torn. The Minnesota valve was replaced, all components were secured, and the unit was tested successfully with no leaks present.

problem: Diesel submersible turbine pump sump contained pockets of water below the boot seals.
solution: Technician removed 2 gallons of water from the sump.

problem: Fueling point 3 card reader was locked and not functioning.
solution: The technician replaced the card reader with a Gen 5 hybrid card reader. After initial replacement, the card reader remained locked until it was flipped upside down, after which fueling point 3 operated correctly. On a subsequent visit, the submersible turbine pump motor and related components were also replaced with a 2 horsepower fixed-speed motor, Guardian box controller, and 40 microfarad capacitor, with calibration and testing confirming proper operation.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Capacitor Assembly for [400170934] ; 1x FFS-FEP 2 HP Pump Motor Assemb [400276906] ; 1x FFS-FEP STP-DHI-SCI, Combo DHI [402312931] ; 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se [W2893895-002]

problem: Fueling point 3 and 4 lost their IGEM programming, causing the dispenser health monitoring to fail.
solution: Technician reprogrammed the IGEM and verified the fueling points came back online and operating normally. No signs of tampering were found.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were dispensing unleaded gasoline very slowly, one penny at a time, while premium grade flowed normally.
solution: Technician replaced the regular grade filter and purged the system, restoring flow rate to 9-10 gallons per minute with no leaks detected. Recommend establishing a filter replacement schedule to prevent future slow-flow issues.

problem: Fueling point 10 hoses (and dispensers 1, 2, 7, 10, 11) had excessive length causing them to hang on the ground, creating a fire code safety hazard.
solution: Not stated

problem: Dispenser software was outdated and required upgrading on the register and controllers; two fueling points had breached security keypads preventing updates.
solution: Upgraded Register and Commander to Base 55.02 and updated all iGems and Jade boards except fueling points 2 and 10 due to compromised SPM keypads. Fueling point 2 also has a broken display that needs replacement; customer was quoted for parts but declined and will address later. Payment network is online and processing successfully at operational pinpads and dispensers.

problem: All fueling points were dispensing fuel at only 6 gallons per minute on all grades due to clogged filters, and the site had no filters installed. Additionally, fueling point 2 had a receipt printer door that would not lock the paper down, and fueling point 8 was printing paper constantly with random codes.
solution: Technician arrived on site and replaced all filters on six dispensers, using twelve filters in total. This restored proper fuel flow rate to 6.5 gallons per minute across all fueling points. Recommend establishing a regular filter replacement schedule to prevent future flow issues.

problem: Fueling point 1 needed a software upgrade and the retail register needed replacement as part of a system modernization project.
solution: Upgraded the Jade board software on fueling point 1 from iXPay version 2.8.205.9 to 2.8.215.6, completed all required configurations, and tested all dispensers successfully. Replaced the existing Topaz register with a new C-18 register, installed a new keyboard, and added a new UPS backup battery for the Commander and supporting equipment. Verified the Commander software version (Base 54.02 / Chevron 1.17.05) did not require upgrading. Noted that a faulty card reader was found on fueling point 4 but advised the customer that another company would handle that repair.
parts: 1x VERIFONE C18 Optional Keyboard [KIT182-030-01-A] ; 1x VERIFONE C18 POS Workstation, [M182-001-01-NAA] ; 1x VERIFONE UPS, Conditioned [P040-07-050]

problem: Fueling point 2 displayed an E01 error after reboot that progressed to a KSB error, and the chip reader was not functional.
solution: Technician tested all systems and confirmed they were operational. The chip reader was tested with a debit chip card and found to be working properly with no issues detected.

problem: Fueling point 5 on the dispenser was not allowing customers to select fuel grades.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 2/7 hold-open latch screws were missing, causing the fueling points to be out of order; hoses on fueling points 2, 3, and 7 were lying on the ground.
solution: Technician repaired all three fueling point hose reels and secured the hoses. The site has replacement screws available in the back office for installation of the hold-open latch screws.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were offline and repeatedly going into a closed state on the price per unit display.
solution: The technician reset the dispenser and moved the security control board to fueling points 5 and 6 to isolate the issue. When the problem did not follow the board to the other dispenser, the technician determined the issue was with the dispenser itself rather than the control board. The technician recommends replacing the IGEM module, which was not available on site and will require a follow-up service visit to complete the repair.

problem: Fueling point 5 and 6 are stopping at random amounts during fueling; fueling point 5 is out of service. The root cause is a code 56 error indicating unexpected movement on the idle meter for premium.
solution: Technician pulled diagnostic codes from the I-GEM system on dispenser 5 and replaced the check valve for premium fuel. The issue has been resolved. Recommend waiving the trip charge for this service call.

problem: The hold-open latch on the nozzles at fueling points 2, 8, 10, and 12 was broken and required replacement.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzles at fueling points 2, 8, 10, and 12 and tested each fueling point to confirm all are in service and flowing normally.

problem: Fueling point pump 1 stops dispensing at exactly $20.16 every time.
solution: Not stated

problem: The Integra automatic tank gauge had a blown channel, causing the dispensers to not communicate properly with the tank monitoring system.
solution: The technician moved the tank probes to a working channel on the Integra and tested all probes to verify they were reading correctly at 100%. Two Ovation bottom door keys were also provided to the customer.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were not dispensing fuel for all grades because the regular shear valve had tripped.
solution: The technician reset the shear valve arm and tested all grades back to 100% functionality on both dispensers. Regular fuel dispensing is now working on fueling points 1 and 2.

problem: After a software upgrade, all fueling points showed poll errors in the evening.
solution: The technician reloaded the SPM software which corrected the initial issue. They discovered the clocks on the edge terminal and pumps were out of synchronization, so they adjusted all clocks to Eastern Standard Time and aligned them with each other, which resolved the polling errors.

problem: The fueling point interface (FCI) box board was not functional, causing the dispensers to not communicate with the pumps.
solution: Replaced the current loop and reset the port to A1-1, which restored pump communication. The equipment is ready for collection.

problem: Fueling point 9 Unihose nozzle had no pressure in the trigger, taking the fueling point out of service.
solution: Technician tested the pump and found it did not engage. Replaced the nozzle with a new one and verified it was working properly by observing customer usage. Fueling point 9 returned to service.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were offline and blinking in a closed state.
solution: The technician disabled F56, opened the dispenser, retrieved error code logs via data dump, and reloaded and reprogrammed the intelligent gateway module (iGem). If the problem persists, replacement of the smart watch module is recommended as the next step.

problem: Fueling point 3 had a cut in the unihose whip hose causing a leak.
solution: The technician replaced the hose on fueling point 3 and tested it.

problem: The card reader on fueling point 1/2 freezes and displays 'one moment please' during transactions.
solution: Not stated

problem: Two hose reels on the dispenser had hoses that needed replacement.
solution: The construction crew replaced two 8-foot hoses and two 50-foot blue hoses on the hose reels.

problem: Controller C18 was not responding and stuck on the loading boot screen, causing all dispensers to be down.
solution: Replaced the C18 controller unit and programmed it. The technician recommends monitoring the new controller for proper operation.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 3 had blank category displays; fueling point 2 also showed a KSB error indicating a breached keypad.
solution: The technician power-cycled fueling point 3, which restored its display to working order. For fueling point 2, the technician replaced the breached keypad, applied debit encryption, returned the dispenser to operational mode, and performed successful testing with no further issues.
Replaced the single-product module on fueling point 2 and tested it to confirm operation.

problem: Dispenser pumps 11 and 12 had a faulty shear valve arm.
solution: Replaced the shear valve arm on both dispensers 11 and 12. After installation, tested both dispensers and confirmed they returned to 100% operational status.
parts: 1x OPW Fusible Link, Safety Hub S [H05361] ; 1x WYN CPU BD, GEN5 WIN10 ANTX UI [WU023682-0001]

problem: Fueling point 3 pinpad displayed a KSB error due to a breached secure payment module.
solution: Technician removed the damaged secure payment module and installed a new IXPay 2.5 model secure payment module. The unit was then injected, tested with a debit card, and confirmed to be operating normally.

problem: Fueling points 13 and 14 were displaying network error and pay inside messages, with fueling point 13 declining all debit transactions and showing a pin pad faulty error; fueling point 15 was showing a KSB error.
solution: The technician replaced the breached submersible turbine pump on fueling point 15 and injected it with PNC bank 400, restoring it to service. For fueling points 13 and 14, the technician found the display control readers were online and working properly after a 3 am reboot. The technician replaced the unresponsive pin pad keypad on the payment terminal at fueling point 13, applied debit key encryption, and performed successful testing to restore normal operation.

problem: Unleaded and premium fueling points were experiencing slow flow rates under 5 gallons per minute due to dirty filters and air in the system, which also caused the leak detector to trip on both tanks.
solution: The technician replaced all filters, purged air from the system, and restored flow rates to 8-10 gallons per minute. Both fueling points returned to normal operation.

problem: Dispenser pump motor was leaking diesel fuel into the above-ground tank.
solution: Technician retightened the bolts on the motor to stop the leak and instructed the maintenance coordinator on how to address it if it leaks again. A second dispenser was found leaking DEF (diesel exhaust fluid), which was noted for monitoring pending parts availability.

problem: Fueling point 4 unihose nozzle was leaking at the break-away connection.
solution: The technician removed the nozzle and cleaned both the nozzle and break-away connection thoroughly, then applied gasoline putty to seal the leak and reinstalled the nozzle. The repair was tested and verified to be leak-free.

problem: Fueling point 9 was offline from the network.
solution: Technician confirmed that fueling point 9 is now online and working properly with the latest IDPOS software version installed. Trip charge was waived.

problem: Dispenser was pumping extremely slowly at approximately 1 gallon per minute.
solution: Technician replaced the fuel filter, which was 18 months old. Flow rate was restored to normal after the filter replacement.

problem: Project installation of C18/Commander system with Chevron software and ATG encountered multiple issues: media (DXPromote) could not communicate with the unit through the Mako box, dispensers lacked proper ethernet connectivity requiring conversion to wireless units, and site electrical infrastructure was inadequate and damaged during installation.
solution: Technician reinstalled DoverAgent and DXMonitor multiple times and verified network configuration using Edge tools, discovering the Mako box was blocking media communication properly. Removed old systems, installed wireless units as alternative to hard-wired ethernet, programmed the Commander and pinpad, mounted fuel control interface, and contacted Mako to confirm configuration and begin Avalan on-boarding. Media installation setup could not be completed due to electrical work interruption and requires a return visit to complete.

problem: Fueling point 2 was declining every other card regardless of which card was used, and both sides of the pump were experiencing slow fuel flow.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 12 nozzle was leaking fuel while customers were pumping.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 12 and tested the flow rate back to 100% to confirm repair was successful.

problem: Register 102 dispenser printer is not working.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 10 nozzle hold-open latch was sticking.
solution: Replaced one nozzle on fueling point 10 and tested the repair.

problem: Fueling point 13 on the Ovation2 Anthem dispenser had no flow.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 11/12 was displaying a closed error due to repeated CANBUS restarts, and fueling point 9 was offline with outdated pricing.
solution: Technician cleared the error with a one-shot code, verified the Jade board CANBUS dip switch settings were correct (A side off, B side on), reseated all related cabling, and restored power to bring fueling point 11/12 back to normal operation. For fueling point 9, technician power cycled the dispenser to reload the card table and confirmed pricing updated to current rates and proper operation.
Not stated

problem: Unleaded fueling point was dispensing at only 4 to 6 gallons per minute instead of the normal 8 to 10 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician changed all filters on the unleaded fueling points and tested the dispensers, restoring flow rate to the correct 8 to 10 gallons per minute.

problem: EVO 200 dispenser was printing a scale test receipt every 4 hours automatically.
solution: Technician reconfigured the scale test print schedule to run once weekly on Sunday morning at 4:00 a.m. instead of every 4 hours, eliminating the unwanted frequent printing.

problem: Fueling point 2 dispenser pump would not come online.
solution: Technician verified that fueling point 2 is now online and operating normally with no issues found. A trip charge was waived.

problem: All fueling points were dispensing unleaded at slow flow, below 5 gallons per minute, with the last filter change performed on April 12, 2026.
solution: Not stated

problem: Diesel nozzle needed replacement, and the fueling points had incorrect drum connectors for the probe wiring.
solution: Replaced overlays and decals on fueling points 3, 4, 7, and 8. Replaced the incorrect drum connectors with the correct ones for the probe wiring. Tested all probes and confirmed they returned to 100% functionality. Customer training was completed.
parts: 4x WYN Silver Decal [365-129999-] ; 4x WAYNE OVATION PID OVERLAY [WU010213-5040-HPW]

problem: The contactless reader on fueling point 2 was breached.
solution: Replaced the contactless reader on fueling point 2 with part number WU024684-0002 and tested multiple transactions to verify the repair. A contactless decal is needed on a return visit to complete the repair.

problem: Fueling point 7 to 12 dispensing regular grade at approximately 2 gallons per minute, which is slower than normal.
solution: Cycled power to the regular grade motor at the affected fueling points and reset the motor breaker, which resolved the slow flow issue. Technician tested the dispenser to confirm normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 1/2 DCR receipt printer is jamming and not feeding paper.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 3/4 was unable to process prepay transactions, with the inside register display showing grey on the pump.
solution: Technician diagnosed a faulty Dover network box inside fueling point 3/4. The network box must be returned and replaced to restore prepay functionality.

problem: Fueling point 1 was dispensing fuel without proper authorization for premium and midgrade, and diesel was overfilling beyond the preset amount.
solution: Technician found the proportional valve for the premium fuel meter was faulty and replaced it. The fuel meter was tested and confirmed to be operating at 100% functionality.

problem: Fueling point 12 nozzle auto shut-off was not engaging properly, causing fuel spills.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on fueling point 12 and tested it. Fueling point 12 returned to service and operating at full flow.

problem: Unleaded fuel dispensers 3 and 4 had slow flow due to clogged filters.
solution: Technician changed the filters on fueling points 3 and 4 using part number 700120. Site was verified to be operating normally; other dispensers confirmed to have full flow.

problem: Slow fuel flow at the unleaded dispensers.
solution: The technician replaced all regulator filters to restore normal flow and cleaned debris from the nozzle boot on fueling point 2. All issues were resolved.

problem: Fueling point 5 whip hose was leaking during use, and fueling points 11 and 12 were showing closed messages. Additionally, the dispenser door security was cycling on and off by itself.
solution: Technician replaced the fueling point 5 hose, which resolved the leak and restored proper operation. Both door sensors were repaired to stop the erratic door security cycling. If the door security issue recurs, all sensors and the door security board should be replaced.

problem: The premium submersible turbine pump motor was running continuously due to a burnt relay.
solution: Replaced the burnt relay and the relay on/off switch. Bypassed the switch, tested the system, and verified that the premium pump now cycles on and off properly.

problem: Dispenser register was running slow with a point-of-sale error displayed.
solution: Technician found that the systems were running optimally and the error was a stuck cosmetic display issue; the dispenser remained fully operational. The system is a RubyCi with upgraded memory and the speed is normal for a base 55 system on older hardware, so no corrective action was needed.

problem: Midgrade and super grade fuel are not dispensing.
solution: Not stated

problem: Dispenser number 2 was missing its overlay decal.
solution: Technician installed the overlay decal on the dispenser. The decal installation was completed at no charge as part of the project, and the dispenser returned to full operation.
parts: 1x WAYNE Sticker, Generic, Contac [WU008347-0002] ; 1x WAYNE Sticker, Generic, Contac [WU008347-0002]

solution: Not stated

problem: The dispenser was not pumping consistently, and gas fumes were venting from a hole below the hose outlet.
solution: The technician found air in the pumping unit and a loose filter. The filter was replaced, the system was tested and purged with 50 gallons back to the tank, restoring flow to 8–10 gallons per minute. The technician recommended that the dealer increase weekly usage through the unit to maintain proper air system function, as the current minimal volume (25–50 gallons per week) may cause air lapses.

problem: Fueling points 2, 6, and 10 had autoshutoff not engaging, causing spills, with flow rates reduced to only 3-5 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician changed all filters on the regular dispensers. Flow rate was restored to 8-10 gallons per minute and all nozzles confirmed working correctly with autoshutoff functioning properly.

problem: Fueling points 1–5 were offline at the register due to incomplete electrical infrastructure from a dock remodeling project and an incorrect pump ID assignment on fueling point 5.
solution: The technician verified that the breaker for fueling points 1–5 had power, corrected the pump ID programming on fueling point 5 to restore it to online status, and determined that an electrician is required to run power wiring from the dock to fueling points 1–4. Additionally, the technician addressed secondary issues discovered on site: replaced a breached SPM keypad on fueling point 7, replaced the card reader on fueling point 13, replaced the UPD on fueling point 4, replaced the QVGA customer display screen on fueling point 5, replaced SPM keypads on fueling points 3 and 4, and adjusted display contrast on fueling point 3.
parts: 1x 8025                      RDM HELIX DISPLAY SALES/VOL WH ; 1x WAYNE Service Kit QVGA Display [892131-001] ; 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se [W2893895-002] ; 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK [W2894472-002-700] ; 3x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK [W2894472-002-700] ; 2x WAYNE Display Module UPD 5 x 1 [WM040824-0007] ; 1x WAYNE Key Pad, Right Softkey, [WU000473-0001] ; 2x WAYNE Display 5.7" QVGA with L [WU004677] ; 1x BOARD, PROGRAMMED, UPD 1-5 [WU016603-0001]

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 main sales display showed "Closed" error and were not displaying store hours, a recurring issue that had occurred three times in two weeks.
solution: The technician rebooted the inside boards on both dispensers, then power cycled the dispensers and enabled function 56, which resolved the display issue and allowed the dispensers to start normally.

problem: All fuel grades at the dispensers were dispensing at slow flow rates, affecting both regular and diesel fueling.
solution: Technician changed all filters on four dispensers (twelve filters total) and tested all grades at all fueling points, restoring full flow. On a follow-up visit, technician discovered the leak detector for regular fuel was leaking from the top and replaced it, returning flow to 100%.
parts: 12x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70120] ; 1x 99 LD2200                   VAPORLESS Leak Detector

problem: All fuel dispensers were rejecting credit card payments and displaying 'pay inside' messages, though the system had been working properly during the day.
solution: Technician contacted Verifone and identified that the Commander payment processor was failing to send data communication records to the Wayne dispensers. Verifone provided and installed a patch to resolve the communication issue, and all systems appeared normal after the update.

problem: Both displays on fueling point 7 and 8 dispenser are stuck on the bootloader screen following a power outage approximately one month ago.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 6 unihose nozzle auto shutoff was not engaging, causing spills.
solution: Replaced the nozzle and tested it with two customer fill-ups to confirm the auto shutoff now engages properly.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 diesel dispensers were delivering slow flow at only 3 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician removed the diesel filters on pumps 1 and 2, installed new filters, and verified the flow rate increased to approximately 7 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 had slow fuel flow, and fueling point 9 had a defective nozzle.
solution: The technician replaced the fuel filter on both fueling points and replaced the bad nozzle on fueling point 9. The line was purged and the system was monitored to confirm the slow flow issue was resolved.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400] ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70120]

problem: Water was present in the fuel tank and needed to be removed from the manhole.
solution: Not stated

problem: Three fueling points were out of service: FP5 had a frozen card reader screen stuck on 'waiting for SCD' that would not reset, FP6's automatic shut-off was not engaging properly causing spills, and FP7 showed 'program memory low' on the card reader screen with unsuccessful reboot attempts.
solution: Replaced the card reader (IXJade) on FP5, wiped and reloaded the software on FP7, and tested both fueling points successfully. For FP6, technician found no mechanical issues and determined the automatic shut-off was functioning properly; the spills were attributed to customer error in operation.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Fuel dispenser site installation was incomplete: atmospheric tank gauge (ATG) probes and diesel float kit were not installed, power was not available to dispensers and back counter, point-of-sale system was not configured, and site was not registered with Verifone.
solution: Technician completed the full installation including: installed two cash registers (C18), thermal printers, scanners, and pinpads with latest Chevron software and Exxon templates; configured Commander site controller and network infrastructure (access point, switch, firewall, media box); installed four service units (SUs) in dispensers with Antix boards and EMV-enabled SPMs; loaded all devices with required software and templates; performed all iGem functions to bring dispensers online with POS; tested debit transactions inside and outside with chip and contactless; and registered site with Verifone. Recommendation: Site manager must contact Verifone to complete final onboarding process.

problem: Fueling point 6 cathode ray tube display was turning pink intermittently.
solution: Technician power cycled the dispenser, inspected and cleaned all display cables. Recommended monitoring the display for recurrence of the discoloration issue.

problem: Fueling point 8 dispenser was reporting an error preventing customers from paying at the pump.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found all systems operating normally with no issues on fueling point 8 or any other dispensers. No corrective action was required.

problem: The dispenser register lost its pairing with the point-of-sale system and could not reconnect because the site manager password was unknown.
solution: The technician forced a password reset to C123456, then successfully re-paired the register with the system. Additionally, the technician disabled PLU printing in the reports configuration, which was causing excessive paper usage during monthly and yearly report printing.

problem: The 12 to 14 foot, 3/4 inch gasoline hose was leaking.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 9 contactless payment reader was not working.
solution: Technician arrived on site, reseated the cable connection and power cycled the EMV reader. After restart, all functions tested normal and the unit came back online.
Not stated

problem: Touch-to-pay payment system was not functioning on dispensers five and six.
