solution: Replaced the touch-to-pay cable assembly connected to the J-board on both dispensers five and six. Tested prepaid and touch-to-pay functions on both units and confirmed all features were working at 100% capacity.

problem: Fueling point 4 nozzle boot was not registering properly, requiring customers to lift and reposition the nozzle multiple times before the pump would activate. Fueling point 5 printer was not printing receipts.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle boot on fueling point 4 and readjusted the printer on fueling point 5. Technician also replaced the hose on fueling point 3. All functions returned to normal operation.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall ; 1x WAYNE Nozzle Boot Plastic PTS

problem: Pump 3 and other fueling points were stuck on loading screen and not responding to register transactions.
solution: Technician downgraded the IXJades software from version 2.8.215.6 to 2.8.212.4, restarted the dispenser control unit driver, and resent the configuration. After testing, the dispenser control unit appeared stable and transactions were processing normally.

problem: Fueling point 3 flapper was stuck and the fueling point was out of service.
solution: Replaced the nozzle boot and tested the fueling point. Fueling point 3 is now back in operation.

problem: Both diesel fueling points were dispensing very slowly, one penny at a time, due to clogged filters.
solution: Replaced both diesel filters on fueling points 1 and 7. Tested the system and confirmed both fueling points are now operating normally.

problem: Unleaded dispenser showing low product level (LR) error and unable to dispense fuel, though tank was not empty; diesel dispenser number 3 on high-speed site also not working.
solution: Found that unleaded motor had been replaced by another service company the previous day without notification—motor and unleaded fuel dispensing were confirmed working properly. For diesel dispenser number 3, identified that the submersible turbine pump wire was connected to the wrong terminal at the junction box in the dispenser, rewired it correctly, and tested all connections; also found the DEF (diesel exhaust fluid) motor had been turned off and reactivated it. All systems tested and confirmed working properly.

problem: Fueling point 5 display controller was frozen on an advertisement screen and unresponsive to reset attempts; fueling point 6 unihose nozzle swivel was leaking.
solution: Technician wiped and reloaded the IXJade display controller in fueling point 5, programmed it, and confirmed normal operation, though noted to replace the IXJade if anomalies recur. For fueling point 6, technician found no issues with the swivel or auto-shutoff function upon testing. Additionally replaced the fuel filter on pump 3/4 which was delivering below 5 gallons per minute and purged the system.

problem: Fueling point 8 hose was leaking, causing the dispenser to be out of service.
solution: Replaced the defective hose and purged the line. Monitored the fueling point during customer use to confirm the issue was resolved.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were showing offline at the dispenser after a power outage, and rebooting from the breaker did not restore them.
solution: The technician found that the IGEM (Interface Gateway Equipment Module) had been wiped during the power outage. The technician reprogrammed the IGEM, verified no tampering had occurred, and confirmed all functions returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were dispensing slower than normal, and the nozzle on fueling point 9 was leaking.
solution: Replaced the filter on fueling points 9 and 10 to restore normal flow rate. Also replaced the leaking nozzle on fueling point 9. All issues were resolved.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread

problem: Fueling point 11 dispenser screen was stuck displaying 'one moment please' with no customer activity, and fueling point 16 was showing 'pay at the pump is currently not available'.
solution: Technician restarted the dispenser, which resolved the stuck message on fueling point 11 and the error on fueling point 16. All fueling points were verified to be operating normally and working properly after the restart.

problem: Pinpad at fueling point register one was not accepting any cards and shutting down frequently.
solution: Technician diagnosed network configuration issues with missing Local Area Network settings and replaced the pinpad at POS 1. Coordinated with Verifone, Mako, and Citgo support to reconfigure loyalty and rewards programs (Club Citgo and FIS), and verified all card transactions (debit and credit) are now processing properly.

Removed old dual RubyCi equipment and installed new dual C18/Commander with M400 pinpad at main register and older M400 relocated to station 102. Programmed commander for Verifone pay Buypass, programmed Skupos payment processing, and tested all payment functions which operated normally.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were dispensing at low flow rate, less than 5 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician changed two filters on the pumps for fueling points 11 and 12 and verified that both pumps returned to full flow operation.

problem: Fueling point 4 regular unleaded grade select button was not working, fueling points 3 and 4 had slow fuel flow, and fueling point 1/2 diesel had reduced flow due to a clogged filter.
solution: Replaced filters on fueling points 3 and 4 for regular unleaded and premium grades, replaced the fueling point 4 regular unleaded grade select button with a new 87 octane overlay, and replaced the clogged diesel filter on fueling point 1/2. All fueling points were verified to be dispensing at approximately 8 gallons per minute after repairs.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 1x WAYNE Decal, Octane Kit (85 Th ; 1x WAYNE Vista Switch CLR Core Gr

problem: Pinpad was blinking on and off, causing the dispenser to go offline.
solution: Replaced the M400 pinpad, loaded it, and tested it with a debit card to verify proper operation.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were offline from the network due to a security breach, fueling points 5 and 6 were dispensing slowly on regular grade, and the gas nozzle on fueling point 12 was stuck and would not shut off.
solution: Technician reloaded the security program on fueling points 11 and 12 and tested all functions back to 100% operational. Replaced the fuel filter on fueling points 5 and 6, restoring flow rate to 8 to 10 gallons per minute. Replaced the gas nozzle on fueling point 12 and verified all dispensers operating at 100%.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Fueling point 7 was displaying a KSB error due to a breached secure PIN entry keypad, and fueling points 5 and 6 were offline in standalone mode.
solution: Replaced the breached keypad on fueling point 7, applied debit key encryption, returned the unit to operational mode, and performed a successful test transaction. Reconnected the 2-wire connection from fueling points 5 and 6 to the FCI current loop board to restore them to online operation.

problem: Atmospheric tank gauge (ATG) was offline and not polling due to a recent storm in the area.
solution: Technician rebooted the power to the ATG, restoring it to full operation. Kent Hamel was contacted to confirm the tank gauge was back online and visible in the monitoring system.

problem: The ATG tank probe on tank #1 was not functioning.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 6 on the Ovation2 Anthem dispenser had a blank screen.
solution: The technician performed a factory reset on fueling point 6, upgraded and reloaded the template, and upgraded the EMV Secure Payment Module. All Anthem fueling point touch screen firmware was reloaded and tested to confirm proper operation.

problem: The regular fuel dispenser nozzle clicked but no fuel dispensed from the fueling point.
solution: The technician diagnosed that the dispenser reset was broken and continuously running without engaging the submersible turbine pump motor. The reset was replaced on fueling point 2 (regular fuel), restoring full dispensing functionality.

problem: Fueling point 3 display screen was frozen and the dispenser showed offline on the DX monitor; power cycling from the breaker did not resolve the issue.
solution: Replaced one jade board, reset the dispenser, and reloaded the software. The technician then configured and tested the fueling point to verify operation.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: Nozzle boot was damaged and taped to the dispenser with missing bracket and destroyed bolt holes; additionally, cards were not working on fueling points 3 and 4.
solution: Replaced the nozzle boot and secured it properly with locking mechanism. Rebooted the Wayne Connect controller inside the dispenser, wiped and reloaded the software on fueling point 3 which was stuck in a boot sequence, and reprogrammed the EMV reader. Tested all functions and confirmed normal operation.

problem: Fueling point register was stuck on Verifone screen and not responding.
solution: Reloaded the register in download mode to fix the IP address, re-paired the devices, and repaired the fuel driver port. All systems were tested and confirmed working.

problem: The automated tank gauge was offline and unable to communicate with the monitoring system.
solution: The technician power cycled the automated tank gauge and then contacted fuel operations, where Eric cleared two stuck alarms that were preventing the system from coming back online.

problem: Multiple fueling points (1/2, 7/8, 9/10) were running at slow flow with auto-shutoff failures, and fueling points 5/6 were offline.
solution: Technician power cycled fueling points 5/6 to restore them to full flow, replaced clogged filters on the slow-flow fueling points, and purged the system. All fueling points returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 2 software is not running properly, preventing customers from scanning the QR code.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 6 unihose auto shut-off was not engaging when it should, causing spills.
solution: Technician tested the nozzle and found no mechanical issue with it. Investigation revealed a software problem preventing customers from scanning the QR code, which was affecting the shut-off function. The pump flow rate and system integrity were verified as normal with no leaks detected.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were not dispensing diesel fuel.
solution: Technician found no issue with the Wayne-branded dispenser itself; the problem was determined to be with the submersible turbine pump (STP). No further corrective action details were documented.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were dispensing diesel at only 4–6 gallons per minute instead of full flow.
solution: The technician changed the diesel filters on both fueling points 7 and 8 and tested twice to confirm full flow was restored. The technician was advised to pull tank bottom samples to investigate the ongoing issue.

problem: Diesel was not dispensing from fueling points 9 and 10.
solution: Technician found a corrupt file in the IGEM (fuel pump controller) on fueling points 9 and 10 and reloaded it, restoring dispensing function. Additionally, technician discovered and corrected multiple configuration issues: turned on canbus switches that were disabled on all B-side dispensers, reseated contactless readers that were plugged into wrong ports on jade boards, and identified that fueling points 5 and 6 are missing the 6-pin to 8-pin connector adapter (part number WU007048-0001) which needs to be installed to complete the repair.

problem: The 42-inch manhole cover lid was broken.
solution: Removed the broken lid pieces and installed a new manhole cover lid.

problem: The battery connection for the overfill alarm on the automatic tank gauge was not functioning properly.
solution: Repaired the battery connection to the overfill alarm and tested the system to verify full functionality. The overfill alarm is now operating at 100%.

problem: Fueling point 6 dispenser was not responding to touch screen input; the ANTX board was corrupted, the screen had leaking glue causing failure, and the pinpad chip reader was damaged.
solution: Technician replaced the corrupted ANTX board, loaded it with the latest software, configured it, and tested the dispenser and EMV functions to confirm proper operation. The pinpad chip reader was also replaced and tested. The customer declined a new screen replacement at that time.
parts: 1x M400 PIN PAD VERIFONE SYSTEMS [M445-403-01-WWA-5] ; 1x WAYNE CPU Board, WIN10, ANTX, [WU020949-0001]

problem: Fueling point 8 was stuck on the startup screen showing 'IDPOS Starting???' and did not come online to the network.
solution: Technician performed a power cycle, then performed an iXReset on the Jade board and reloaded the latest IDPOS software. The Sage card dispenser was automatically updated, the Jade board was reconfigured, and fueling point 8 was allowed to fully initialize. A test transaction was completed successfully to verify the fix.

problem: Fueling points 1–10 were dispensing Regular gasoline at low flow rates of 4–6 gallons per minute instead of full flow.
solution: Technician found that pumps 5, 6, 11, and 12 for Diesel were also running below full flow and replaced filter part number 70120 on both dispensers. Technician recommends sending a specialist to pull fuel samples from the bottom of the tank to check for contamination, as this is an ongoing issue.

problem: Fueling points 3-12 were dispensing Regular gasoline at reduced flow rates of 4-6 gallons per minute instead of normal speed.
solution: Technician replaced all six Regular fuel filters on the dispensers and verified premium was operating normally. After filter replacement, all dispensers were tested and confirmed to be flowing at the normal rate of 8-10 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were dispensing unleaded fuel at slow flow, less than 5 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician changed the fuel filter on fueling points 1 and 2, and tested the fuel flow. Both positions returned to full flow operation. Fueling points 11 and 12 remain closed due to software issues.

problem: Automatic tank gauge (ATG) system was outdated and needed replacement; old TS750 Incon ATG unit required an upgrade.
solution: Replaced the old TS750 Incon ATG with a new EVO 200 unit with printer, installed it on the wall, programmed it, performed test print, and confirmed proper operation. Reconnected and configured the PDS Solutions box for Veeder Root compatibility and verified tank level communication.
parts: 1x ATG 6 CH, DISPLAY W/TANK TEST [EVO200D-T] ; 1x FMP-ETP                 EVO 200/400 EXTERNAL PRINTER

problem: Fueling point 6 display screen is blank, register 102 pinpad is not accepting Mastercard, and register 102 printer is not working.
solution: Swapped printers between register 101 and 102 to resolve the printer issue. Reloaded pinpad 127 to fix the card acceptance problem. Fueling point 6 requires jade board replacement, which the customer declined at this time.

problem: The submersible turbine pump sump sensor on dispenser L06 was defective, causing a sensor open alarm.
solution: The technician replaced the defective sump sensor and tested the tank monitor to verify all functions returned to normal with no alarms present.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 were flashing closed with no store hours displayed, preventing customer access even after the site attempted a reset.
solution: The technician disabled door security function 56 and rebooted the dispenser unit. The fueling points are now fully operational with successful transaction tests completed. Note: This was the third service visit for this issue; recommend escalation to vendor if the problem recurs, as it is covered under the APEC warranty.

problem: The submersible turbine pump had an open circuit caused by a failed motor, yoke, and smart controller board that had all shorted out.
solution: Replaced the motor, yoke, and smart controller board. After replacement, all functions returned to normal and the pump was tested successfully with multiple fill-ups. Full Advanced Petroleum Equipment Company warranty applied.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Wire connector kit (In [400200930] ; 1x FFS-FEP 1-1/2 HP Pump Motor [400276903] ; 1x FFS-FEP STP-DHI-SCI, Combo DHI [402312931]

problem: The nozzle at fueling point 5 was repeatedly clicking off during fueling due to a broken auto shut-off tube inside the spout.
solution: Replaced the faulty nozzle and performed a test transaction. The unit is now operating properly with no leaks.

problem: The diesel tank's submersible turbine pump probe was reporting incorrect water levels (24 inches of water, approximately 600 gallons), but a manual stick check revealed no water was actually present in the tank and the actual level was 21.7 inches.
solution: The technician swapped the probe from the premium tank to the diesel tank to isolate whether the problem was with the probe or the tank sensor. After the swap, all readings returned to normal (100% operational). The technician instructed the dealer to monitor the premium tank; if an alarm triggers on that tank, it would confirm the probe is faulty since that probe was originally in the diesel tank.

problem: Site required scheduled software upgrade on multiple systems including Jade board and supervisor module across 10 fueling points.
solution: Technician shut down all pumps and registers, backed up the system from the Commander, then successfully upgraded Ruby 2 registers, Commander, iGem, and Jade board. Applied network configurations, reloaded loyalty settings, initialized fuel systems and DCRs, and verified operation with a successful test card transaction.

problem: Both the unleaded and diesel submersible turbine pump sumps contained pockets of water below the boot level.
solution: The technician removed 4 gallons of water from the sumps.

problem: Card reader on fueling point 10 was not working.
solution: Technician replaced the card reader on fueling point 10 and tested it. Technician also reconnected the breakaway on fueling point 1 and purged the line to address related issues.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: Fueling point 6 universal hose nozzle was reported leaking and the pump was out of service.
solution: Technician arrived and found fueling point 6 open and fueling point 5 closed. Tested fueling point 5 and confirmed it operated as intended, then reopened it. Inspected fueling point 6 and found no spills, leaks, or issues present.

problem: The fueling point was vapor locking intermittently and releasing excessive fuel fumes from the hose connection, preventing consistent fuel dispensing.
solution: The technician replaced the cracked hose, old swivel, and damaged nozzle, then tested and purged the fueling line to restore proper operation.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall [08C124291FL] ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400]

problem: AX12 touch screen was not responding correctly.
solution: Not stated. The dispatch notes provided instructions for recalibration (downloading software, ordering programming cable P/N WU025582, and calibrating fueling points), but the corrective action notes do not indicate whether these steps were actually completed or what the technician found and fixed on site.

problem: Diesel tank probe showed invalid reading and would not count fuel level due to disconnected probe cable inside the seal pack.
solution: Technician cleaned the wires and reconnected the diesel tank probe cable inside the seal pack, then resealed it. All functions returned to normal operation.

problem: Multiple fueling points were malfunctioning: pump 2 was not dispensing fuel after prepay with amounts returning to register, pump 3 credit card reader was not accepting cards, pumps 5 and 8 were out of service.
solution: Replaced one submersible unit on fueling points 5 and 6, replaced two nozzle boots on fueling points 2 and 3, and repaired the template on fueling point 8. All four fueling points are now operational.
parts: 2x WAYNE Nozzle Boot Plastic PTS [WU003091-0002] ; 1x WAYNE MODULE, DFS WIRELESS IN- [WU019855-0001]

problem: Fueling point 11 was displaying offline and rebooting repeatedly, with business hours not showing on the display.
solution: The technician identified a software issue in the IXJade controller, wiped and reloaded the software, then reprogrammed the unit. The fueling point returned to normal operation and tested successfully.

problem: Fueling point 11 nozzle handle auto shut-off was not engaging and the trigger was sticking, preventing manual shut-off, with a leak reported at the site.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on dispenser 11 after finding the flapper was stuck. Tested the dispenser and confirmed it returned to full operating condition.

problem: Fueling point 4 nozzle auto shutoff was not engaging, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: Replaced the broken nozzle. Tested the fueling point and confirmed it is now operating at 100%.

problem: The dispenser remote control required to program and change fuel prices was not received with the equipment.
solution: Not stated
Not stated

problem: Vapor recovery cap was missing from the RUL vapor recovery system, and the ATG high tank alarm was showing an error.
solution: Replaced the missing vapor recovery cap and tested the system to confirm it was functioning at 100%. Cleared the ATG high tank alarm error to restore normal operation.
parts: 1x OPW Top Seal Vapor Cap, EVR

problem: Fueling point 1 submersible turbine pump was not communicating with the monitor system, fueling point 11 nozzle valve stop was not working correctly and caused slow flow, and all 12 site filters were clogged.
solution: Rebooted the system to restore FP1 pump communication. Replaced the FP11 nozzle and installed new filters at all 12 locations on site; fueling now operates at normal rate. Replaced the 15-inch manhole cover.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 12x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread ; 1x 15" EBW LID

problem: Fueling point 8 stops dispensing mid-sale for all fuel grades.
solution: Technician arrived on site and identified a damaged nozzle as the cause. The customer declined to authorize out-of-pocket repair service.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were dispensing all grades of fuel at slow flow rate, less than 5 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician arrived on site and replaced all three filters on fueling points 7 and 8. Fuel flow returned to normal operating rate.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 11 card reader display was blank and unresponsive; reboot attempts did not resolve the issue.
solution: Technician found the display stuck on boot loader startup screen. Reloaded both the SCD and Jade board software, reconfigured the Jade board, and performed a system reboot. Test transaction completed successfully after restoration.

problem: New point-of-sale and fueling point system installation required at site with four AX12 dispensers and one Global dispenser.
solution: Installed C18/Commander system with latest BP Buypass software, Mako box, and network switch; established communication with BP/Amoco network and all dispensers; programmed BP Earnify Loyalty and MobilePay with testing completed; verified debit functionality; confirmed proper nozzle color coding (black for diesel, blue for unleaded); completed system onboarding and documentation.

problem: Multiple dispenser issues across four fueling points: Ruby2 register stuck in continuous reboot loop with corrupted SATA DOM; FP 3/4 displaying 'Please Pay Inside' error with failed communication; FP 4 showing 'Keypad Unavailable' and 'Closed' errors with faulty UPD board; FP 2 SPM keypad displaying KSB error.
solution: Replaced corrupted SATA DOM in register and reloaded software. Swapped 485 communication unit in FP 3/4 and rebooted. Replaced SPM keypads on FP 2 and FP 4, injected debit key encryption, and verified operation. Identified defective UPD board on FP 4 through board-swap testing, then replaced PPU boards on FP 3/4 on final visit. Performed test transactions to prime fuel lines and confirmed all dispensers operating normally.
parts: 1x RDM New Style Pump Relay Board ; 1x Verifone Sata Dom 2Gb OS 3.11. ; 1x WAYNE Connect Kit IN Dispenser ; 2x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK ; 1x WAYNE Display, Module UPD 5 x

problem: Preventive maintenance inspection of hanging hardware components (hoses, nozzles, swivels, breakaways) at all fueling points to check for expiration dates, damage, and proper functionality.
solution: Technician inspected all five fueling points and found all hoses in good condition with no dragging or damage. Checked expiration dates on swivels (all expiring 06/27 or 08/28) and nozzles (all expiring 09/30), verified breakaways in good condition, and confirmed retractor clamps and cables were fully functional. No components required replacement at this time.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 4 were dispensing regular and premium gasoline very slowly at approximately 2 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician found and replaced the broken shear valve arm on dispenser 3/4 regular, and replaced the premium tank probe lid (18-inch black manhole cover). All fueling points were restored to full 100% flow rate.
parts: 1x OPW Fusible Link, Safety Hub S

problem: The receipt printers on fueling points 1, 3, and 9 were not working due to paper jams and printer errors.
solution: The technician attempted to clear the paper jams on the first visit but was unsuccessful, so all three printers were replaced on a return visit. The technician replaced the printers on fueling points 1 and 9, and reloaded the circuit board on fueling point 3 to complete the repair.

problem: Chevron network was offline, causing payment processing to fail with outside debit working only and inside credit working only.
solution: Technician identified an internet connectivity issue at the site with 36% packet loss. Site was temporarily restored using a hotspot and tested. Customer was advised they need improved internet service from their provider, and a separate ticket was created for a required software upgrade on both inside and outside systems.

problem: Pump #8 is not accepting card payments, and both diesel and premium sides have slow flow issues.
solution: Technician found the diesel shear valve was closed but allowing fuel through, causing slow flow, and recommended replacing the diesel shear valve on the 1/2 line. Premium filter was changed on the 1/2 line, but further inspection revealed a construction-side issue requiring handoff to the construction team for rectification.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP MagVFC , 2 HP or 4 HP [5874202800] ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70120]

problem: Automatic tank gauge (ATG) monitor was offline due to a broken USB clip on the Integra 100 printer and disconnected WiFi connection after customer installed new WiFi.
solution: Rerouted the printer cable to a different USB port to bypass the broken clip, restoring printer functionality. Contacted MyTankInfo support to reconnect the WiFi to the Integra 100 tank monitor, bringing the system back online and operational.

problem: Fueling points 5 and 6 were out of service and displaying slow, inconsistent flow rates on both regular and premium grades.
solution: Technician tested fueling points 5 and 6, confirmed suboptimal flow rates on both grades, replaced both fueling points, purged the lines, and reopened them for service. All other pumps were verified to be operating normally.

problem: Card reader on fueling point 1 was displaying an error message 'One moment please' and then returning to the idle screen, preventing fuel dispensing and causing intermittent transaction failures.
solution: Technician replaced the card reader and verified all connections were properly secured. Multiple tests confirmed the reader is now functioning correctly and customers can proceed with transactions and dispense fuel.
parts: 1x WAYNE Anthem Card Reader [W2894103-002] ; 1x WAYNE Display, Module UPD 5 x [WM040824-0011]

problem: Fueling point 6 unihose nozzle was leaking fuel during use, and fueling points 9 and 10 were dispensing regular and premium grades at reduced flow rates (slower than 5 gallons per minute).
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 6 to stop the fuel leak and replaced the filters on fueling points 9 and 10 to restore proper flow rates. All issues were resolved.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 2x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread

problem: Fueling point 3 push-to-start buttons were not engaging or lighting up, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: The technician lubricated the flapper on the nozzle holder to resolve the issue.

problem: Diesel leak detector needed to be replaced during tank conversion.
solution: The diesel leak detector was replaced with a gas leak detector.

problem: Fueling point 2/5 EMV card reader was stuck displaying 'sales completed thank you/unavailable' message and would not clear after reboot attempt, causing the pumps to be taken out of service.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found fueling points 2 and 5 operating in full working order with no issues present. The card reader had cleared and was functioning normally; no further corrective action was required.
Not stated

problem: Diesel exhaust fluid (DEF) would not dispense from the new dispenser after the first delivery was received.
solution: Technician opened the shut-off ball valve at the submersible turbine pump, moved the hook relay from slot 4 to slot 5 on both dispensers, and verified purge function. Dispenser returned to 100% operation.

problem: Tank 1 pump 1 was only dispensing 0.1 gallons before shutting off, and the nozzle on fueling point 1 was leaking.
solution: Replaced the damaged breakaway coupling and the leaking nozzle on fueling point 1. All issues were resolved.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400] ; 1x CENTRAL Breakaway, ", Catlow [CTM75]

problem: Fueling points 5 and 6 were dispensing fuel very slowly.
solution: The technician arrived on site and tested both fueling points by closing them down individually and checking fuel flow, grades, fuel pressure, and electrical systems. All systems tested normal and full flow was confirmed. The issue did not recur during testing. The technician reopened all fueling points and the slow-flow problem did not return.

problem: A customer drove away with the nozzle still attached to fueling point 2 regular dispenser.
solution: Not stated
Technician removed debris from the dispenser for $700; however, the specific location of the debris and the impact on pump operation were not documented.

problem: Preventative maintenance inspection of hanging hardware components at six fueling points to check for expired or damaged parts.
solution: Technician inspected all fueling points and found that hoses, breakaways, and swivels were generally in good condition. A broken hose retriever at fueling point 2 was identified as needing replacement. Several nozzles with expiration dates in the next 6 months were noted (fueling points 2, 4, and 5) and should be scheduled for replacement.

problem: The remote underground location submersible turbine pump sump contained pockets of water below the boot seals.
solution: The technician removed 2 gallons of water from the sump. Corrosion should be removed from the submersible turbine pump and the equipment should be painted to prevent future corrosion.

problem: The manhole tank cover was broken after a customer drove over it.
solution: Installed a replacement OPW Conquistador 39 ½ inch manhole lid.

problem: Fueling point 2 Unihose nozzle auto shutoff was not engaging, causing multiple overfills.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on fueling point 2 and tested it to confirm proper operation with no overfills and full flow pumping.

problem: Submersible turbine pump sump contained pockets of water below the boot seals.
solution: Technician removed 2 gallons of water from the sump.

problem: The premium tank manhole lid was missing or damaged and needed replacement.
solution: Replaced the probe lid on the premium tank with an 18-inch black manhole cover. All equipment is now operational.

problem: Fueling point 7 nozzle auto shut-off was constantly engaged and would not disengage, causing the dispenser to be out of service. Fueling point 2 had the same issue.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 7 and the nozzle on fueling point 2. Both dispensers were tested and returned to 100% functionality. Flow rate was verified at 8 to 10 gallons per minute on both units.

problem: Fueling point 4 had a jammed receipt printer causing gibberish output, and fueling point 8 displayed 'Out of Service' due to a breached SPM keypad.
solution: Cleared the paper jam from the fueling point 4 printer, tested it to confirm proper operation, and educated staff on proper door closure to prevent future jams. For fueling point 8, replaced the breached SPM keypad, injected debit key encryption, reloaded firmware via Jade board, and tested to restore full dispenser operation.

problem: The nozzle boot and breakaway assembly on fueling point 5 were damaged or worn and needed replacement.
solution: Not stated
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread [70060] ; 1x WAYNE Nozzle Boot Plastic PTS [WU003091-0002]

problem: Fueling point 1 and 2 were dispensing at a slow flow rate of less than 5 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician arrived on site, assessed the fueling points, and replaced the filters. Both fueling points returned to normal flow rates and full operation.

problem: Dispenser pump 2 was dispensing fuel after prepay but the transaction was not posting to the register correctly, showing amount due instead of completed sale, with DCR poll errors reported.
solution: Technician found the site terminal ID for BP Mobile had been deleted from the commander. Reinstalled BP Mobile configuration and verified all dispenser control readers were fully functional and online with normal operation restored. No return trip charge applied.

problem: Dispensers 3 and 4 were offline from the register, and inside and outside dispenser systems needed software upgrades.
solution: Technician upgraded outside fueling points with latest ANTX and SPM2 software, upgraded inside dispenser systems, and reprogrammed all equipment. After contacting BP to confirm table updates, technician downloaded EMV updates for inside and outside dispensers, tested all card functions and loyalty program settings, and confirmed all systems operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 2 universal hose nozzle handle was not releasing properly, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on fueling point 2. The pump is now working properly.

problem: Fueling point 2 card reader was not accepting card insertions.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling points 6 and 2 were shutting off mid-sale after pumping only a few cents, then returning prepay transactions to the customer.
solution: Technician diagnosed error codes indicating solenoid valve and sensor failures. Replaced the premium solenoid, check valve, relief valve, and premium fuel filter on fueling point 6; replaced the regular unleaded solenoid, check valve, relief valve, and both regular and premium filters on fueling point 2. All fueling points tested successfully at approximately 8 gallons per minute with no leaks. Recommend returning to replace both valves on fueling point 6 regular unleaded if the issue persists.

problem: Fueling point 6 had a leaking nozzle swivel due to an insecurely fastened breakaway coupling.
solution: Technician secured the breakaway coupling on fueling point 6 and tested the dispenser to confirm proper operation at 100%. Note: The problem was on dispenser 6, not dispenser 12 as initially reported.

problem: Regular and premium fuel dispensers were delivering less than 5 gallons per minute (GPM) flow rate.
solution: Technician rebuilt pressure on the regular fuel line to restore normal flow rate. Premium fuel pressure was already adequate. Flow was monitored to confirm the issue was resolved.
Not stated

problem: The tank monitor was configured to run weekly leak tests instead of daily, which caused the regular tank to fail the test because high fuel throughput was interfering with test accuracy.
solution: Reconfigured the tank monitor to run daily leak tests for both the regular and premium tanks, which provides better test stability and allows the tanks to pass the tests more reliably.

problem: Fueling point 3 automatic shutoff nozzle was not engaging properly.
solution: Technician replaced the defective nozzle on fueling point 3 and cleaned debris from the nozzle boots on fueling points 3 and 4 to restore proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 8 was displaying a "Pay Inside" error and the card reader was stuck in an initializing state.
solution: The technician placed all devices (keypad, contactless reader, and card reader) into maintenance mode and then returned them to operational mode. The technician reloaded the DCR (device configuration) at the register for fueling point 8.

problem: Fueling point 5 card reader was frozen displaying 'waiting on SCD' message, causing the fueling point to be out of service.
solution: Technician arrived on site and observed the unit was fully functional with no issues detected. The dispenser reboot performed by the site resolved the problem.

problem: Topaz register #102 was printing receipts slowly and the printer was not responding, showing 'Printer Unavailable' error.
solution: Technician corrected the baud rate from 19200 to 9600, reassigned the printer from COM7 to COM4, and rebooted the register. After identifying a faulty printer by swapping it with another register, the technician replaced the printer and verified the register is now operating normally with successful print functionality.

problem: Routine preventative maintenance inspection of all hanging hardware components at eight fueling points to check for expired parts, damage, and proper functionality.
solution: Technician inspected all eight fueling points and their hoses, swivel/breakaway assemblies, and nozzles. All components were found to be in good condition with no expired parts, damage, leaking, cracking, or functional issues identified; all retractor clamps and cables were fully operational.

problem: The dispenser pump unit has worn gears that are grinding internally, causing the pump to be inoperative.
solution: The technician identified the worn gear issue and initiated a replacement of the pumping unit (model 9153A, serial number 500243). A new unit was ordered and priced with the dealer. Additional documentation photos were provided to support the replacement request.

problem: Fueling point 7 dispenser has no power on the A side after a customer service representative pulled the printer to change paper, preventing fuel grade selection. Additionally, fueling point 5 has a non-responsive push-to-start panel that does not recognize any of the four product selections, and fueling point 7 may have a corrupted iGem unit.
solution: Technician identified that power loss on the A side of fueling point 7 (B12 model, serial 81611C) requires a fuse board replacement, and the iGem may need replacement if corrupted by the power loss. Fueling point 5 (B23 model, serial 81623C) requires a push-to-start panel board for four-product operation. Technician will return with both the fuse board and push-to-start panel board to complete repairs.

problem: The regular 87 tank probe at the ATG tank monitor was not working, with a blown thermister channel on tank 3 and intermittent reading issues.
solution: The technician replaced the probe board in the tank monitor and repaired a loose wire at the junction box on the tank 3 probe. All four tanks were tested and confirmed reading correctly after the repairs. The dealer was advised to monitor the probe due to its history of intermittent problems, and a replacement probe was ordered to keep in stock.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 10 was offline from the network and stuck on the startup screen displaying 'IDPOS Starting???' without progressing.
solution: Performed a power cycle, then performed an iXReset on the Jade board and reloaded the latest IDPOS software. Completed an automatic update of the SCD, reconfigured the Jade board, and allowed the fueling point to fully initialize. Verified the fix with a successful test transaction.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were not reading any cards.
solution: The technician tested both card readers on fueling points 1 and 2 and found them operating properly with no faults detected. Both fueling points were opened for public use and monitored; multiple customers successfully completed transactions using both card payments and Scan & Go with no issues, indicating the problem had resolved.

problem: Pinpad 102 was not reading chip cards or contactless payments, and register 102 was not reading product lookup codes (PLUs), both issues occurring after a power outage.
solution: The technician replaced pinpad 102 (covered under warranty) and reloaded the register 102 software, which resolved both the pinpad and register reading issues. Both devices were tested and confirmed working with debit card chip and contactless payments.

problem: Unleaded fuel filters needed replacement at all fueling points except 7-10.
solution: Technician replaced all regular unleaded filters at the requested fueling points using three filters provided by the site. Tested all dispensers for leaks and purged the system; all functions returned to normal.

problem: Scanner cable was missing from the scanner equipment.
solution: Provided customer with scanner cable part #90A051939, which is a standard component that should come with the scanner. No charge was applied.
Installed a 2 horsepower motor and 40 microfarad capacitor to resolve the interface problems. Motor and capacitor are working as expected.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Capacitor Assembly for ; 1x FFS-FEP 2 HP Pump Motor Assemb

problem: Tank probe 87 for unleaded was not showing on the tank monitor and regular readings were not displaying.
solution: Investigation revealed the probe was functioning correctly and showing an accurate low-level alarm because the regular tank contained only 17 inches of fuel. The facility was advised to order fuel to bring tank levels back to normal operating range. The system is now working properly.

problem: The tank monitor application was not working and the screen was stuck because the SMTP settings were missing and the ProGuage programming was incorrect.
solution: The technician reprogrammed the tank monitor with correct settings and attempted to reset the empty-to-go function, but was unsuccessful. The technician needs to return to set up tank communications properly, as the previous technician did not program it correctly.

problem: The fueling point 5 Tank 1 regular unleaded interstitial space sensor was in alarm condition due to a failed annular float sensor; additionally, fueling point 10 had a nozzle that was overflowing.
solution: The technician replaced the faulty Tank 1 interstitial sensor and tested it to confirm proper operation. On fueling point 10, the technician replaced the bad nozzle. The technician also replaced a temporary bell sensor on Tank 3/4 premium/diesel interstitial with the correct interstitial sensor, routing approximately 50 feet of 1/16-inch stainless steel cable as a pull rope around the double-wall tank; the sensor was tested and is working correctly.
parts: 2x VEEDER-ROOT Interstitial Senso ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded

problem: Fueling point 8 was stuck on the startup screen displaying 'IDPOS Starting???' and could not progress or reset, leaving it out of service.
solution: The technician power cycled the dispenser, performed an iXReset on the Jade board, and reloaded the latest IDPOS software. The Jade board was reconfigured, the SCD was automatically updated, and the fueling point was allowed to fully initialize. A test transaction was then successfully processed to verify the repair.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were dispensing fuel at a slow rate of 4-6 gallons per minute instead of the normal 8 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced the fuel filter on fueling points 9 and 10. After replacement, flow rate testing confirmed that regular unleaded was dispensing at the correct rate of approximately 8 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling point 4 was stuck on the welcome screen with the card reader not working, causing the pump to be offline.
solution: The technician performed a dispenser reboot, which resolved the freeze screen issue. Chip transactions were tested and confirmed successful.

problem: Pump 5 was not pumping at all and pump 2 was not pumping diesel; additionally, dispensers 6 and 9 were missing display board part WU020250-0001 and other installation equipment including ethernet cables and DFS was not installed.
solution: Technician added ends to internal ethernet cables, inspected equipment, had a new technician complete ethernet cable terminations and fix related issues, and began checking commander programming. Installation of missing display board and DFS was not completed; recommend sending technician to complete installation of display board part WU020250-0001, DFS, and verify pump operations after commander programming is finished.
parts: 1x WAYNE Board, Pump Relay [WU007116-0001] ; 1x Wayne Display Board, Programme [WU020250-0001]

problem: The tank monitor (ProGauge) was displaying incorrect future dates on test results, and the optical sump sensor was damaged and crushed.
solution: Replaced the broken optical sump sensor with a standard OPW 30-0231-L non-optical sensor. Reprogrammed the ProGauge to display correct current dates on test results and adjusted the tank monitor settings to show daily readings instead of weekly, monthly, or yearly readings (change to take 36-48 hours to fully process).

problem: All fueling points were running slow at approximately 5 gallons per minute, with fueling points 1, 2, and 5 unable to dispense due to air in the fuel system and clogged filters.
solution: The technician replaced all filters and purged air from the fuel system. After service, all fueling points were restored to normal flow rate of approximately 9 gallons per minute.

problem: Topaz register memory was at 95% capacity, preventing End of Day processing, report generation, and prepay dispenser functionality because the Roll-Up feature was not enabled and transaction logs were not being properly saved to the Commander system.
solution: Performed full reload of Commander and Topaz registers with latest Base 55 version, installed additional 4GB RAM module, cleaned up storage, and reconfigured system parameters. Recommendation: Enable Roll-Up feature and maintain regular system maintenance to prevent future memory capacity issues.
parts: 1x VERIFONE Commander Memory Modu [ICM00131] ; 1x GRYPHON Cable, Barcode Scanner [VF-DTL4410-6]

problem: All fueling points were dispensing at slow flow rate, with regular grade fuel flowing at only 5 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician replaced the filters at the site, which restored the flow rate to normal. The site was monitored and no further issues were observed.

problem: Multiple dispensers at the site were not responding—fueling points 8, 5, 11, and 12 had grayed-out screens, would not pump, and could not select grades or display prices; three of four diesel pumps were down.
solution: Not stated

problem: Nozzle was pulled from the hose at fueling point 6.
solution: The technician reattached the breakaway coupling and tested both fueling points 6 and 11 to confirm they were operating at full flow.

problem: Fueling points 4/5 and 5/6 were dispensing fuel very slowly at approximately 3 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician replaced two filters for unleaded fuel and tested the pump, which then dispensed at full flow rate of 8.5 to 9 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling point 1 nozzle hold-open latch was sticking and auto shutoff did not engage.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on fueling point 1 (the initial replacement nozzle was defective, so a second replacement was installed). After installation, the technician purged the line and monitored for leaks to verify proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 4 dispenser display was blank, and fueling point 2 keypad showed a KSB error indicating the keypad was breached.
solution: For fueling point 4, performed an iXReset on the Jade board, reloaded the latest iXPay software, configured the board, rebooted the dispenser, and allowed it to download DCR from the register. For fueling point 2, replaced and secured the breached keypad, applied debit key encryption, loaded EMV/DCR, and returned the unit to operational mode. Both fueling points passed test transactions.

problem: Two AX 12 dispensers needed to be upgraded to ANTXs with new settlement processors and payment terminals installed along with a Verifone point-of-sale system.
solution: Upgraded two AX 12 dispensers to ANTXs with new settlement processors, installed two fuel point units, installed one C18 controller with site controller and loaded generic buypass software, installed and paired access point, loaded pinpad with kernel package, injected debit encryption key into pinpad and settlement processors, and performed successful table download and debit card testing both indoors and outdoors. Technician notes that follow-up visit is needed to complete media configuration, autolinking setup, customer site management onboarding, and system registration.

problem: Fueling point 5 was not dispensing gas and the touch screen was not responding.
solution: Cycled power to the touch control board, which resolved the touch screen issue. Re-paired the authorization point with the system unit to fix a communication problem, then tested the fueling point to confirm operation.

problem: Diesel nozzle was damaged when a customer drove off with it still connected, causing the fueling point to no longer connect properly.
solution: Replaced the fueling point breakaway assembly and nozzle. Recommend customer awareness signage about properly disconnecting nozzles before driving away.

problem: Fuel filters on diesel pump 1/2 needed replacement.
solution: Technician replaced the fuel filters on the diesel pump. After replacement, the pump was tested and confirmed to be running at full flow.

problem: Fueling point 6 unihose flapper was sticking, preventing fuel from dispensing.
solution: Technician inspected the pump nozzle boot to ensure nothing was interfering with the flapper, then lubricated the flapper with WD-40. The flapper is now operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 4 kiosk was offline, taking all pumps out of service.
solution: Gilbarco technical assistance center remotely cleared corrupted electronic payment system files that were preventing the fueling point from coming online. The entire system was rebooted and a test transaction confirmed normal operation.

problem: The ATG tank probe was reading incorrectly, showing levels off by several hundred gallons, and printers on fueling points 1 and 3 were not printing receipts.
solution: Checked both ATG probes and floats, recalibrated the ATG system, and adjusted tank levels. Manually confirmed all levels were correct and verified both printers were functioning by printing test receipts from both fueling points.

problem: Hose reel needed to be changed at the fuel dock.

problem: Fuel was leaking from the premium nozzle.
solution: Replaced the premium nozzle. The equipment is now operating normally.

problem: Preventative maintenance inspection of hanging hardware components at all fueling points to check for expired parts, damage, and proper functionality.
solution: Technician inspected all dispensers and fueling positions, examining hoses, swivels, nozzles, and breakaway assemblies at each fueling point. problem: Routine preventive maintenance inspection of hanging hardware components at fueling point 1, including hoses, retractors, clamps, and cables for expiration dates, damage, leaks, or safety concerns.
solution: Technician inspected fueling point 1 hanging hardware and determined the hose appears to be in acceptable condition with no immediate issues requiring replacement.

problem: The diesel tank probe was reading inaccurately, 0.75 inches lower than the actual product level, and the maximum product alarm would not trigger during ATG testing.
solution: The technician found that the probe float kit was positioned at 95% of its measuring range instead of the proper level. The technician adjusted the tank offset settings on the tank monitor and repositioned the float kit, which restored the probe to 100% functionality and enabled the high alarm to activate correctly.

problem: Dispenser point-of-sale system needed migration from Marathon to Fiserv payment processing, including pinpad and card reader updates.
solution: Technician reloaded the Verifone register with latest Marathon Fiserv software, injected Fiserv debit keys into the pinpad and outside card readers, and verified Marathon Rewards and MobilePay functionality. All network and payment systems were tested and confirmed online; site registration was completed but customer did not provide credentials needed for final onboarding.

problem: Fueling points 8, 9, and 10 receipt printer doors were sticking and not unlocking to change the printer.
solution: Technician checked and repaired all printer door locks on the affected fueling points. Additionally, fueling point 7 was found frozen; cycling power resolved that issue. All fueling points tested and confirmed running as expected.

problem: Fuel station point-of-sale system required software upgrade from Marathon to Fiserv.
solution: Reloaded Verifone register with latest Marathon Fiserv software, reconfigured LAN and network settings, downloaded loyalty and mobile payment tables, and reinjected pinpad and all card readers with Fiserv debit keys. Site was registered with Verifone and all systems tested successfully. C-Site Management onboarding was not completed because customer did not provide credentials and was not on-site.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 12 card readers were unavailable with breached SPM keypads, causing the dispensers to display out of service.
solution: Replaced the breached SPM keypads on both dispensers, injected the debit encryption keys, reloaded the Jade board, and returned both fueling points to operational mode. All functions were tested and confirmed working correctly.

problem: Preventative maintenance inspection of hanging hardware components at fueling point 1, including hoses, retractor clamps, and cables.
solution: Technician inspected the hose and found it to be in acceptable condition with no signs of leaking, cracking, holes, or damage. No corrective action was required at this fueling point.

problem: Fueling point 11 was offline from the network and non-operational; fueling point 2 nozzle was leaking.
solution: Upgraded the Jade board and ISCD software to the latest version, performed configuration, and tested the dispenser successfully to confirm transactions. Replaced the leaking nozzle on fueling point 2.

problem: The o-ring on the dispenser meter failed, causing the top and bottom sections of the meter to separate.
solution: The technician replaced the o-ring and installed new fasteners to rejoin the meter sections. After testing with several vehicles, no leaks were detected and the dispenser was operating normally at 17 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were dispensing fuel at a very slow rate due to a dirty filter.
solution: The technician replaced the dirty filter and purged the fuel line. The site was monitored to confirm the issue was resolved.

problem: The fuel hose swivel and nozzle on the dispenser were leaking, and the tank product level was very low.
solution: Replaced the swivel and nozzle on the dispenser and tested the equipment to ensure it was operating at 100% functionality.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400] ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x [241TPS-0241]

problem: The back office system (Mercury One) was offline, causing the entire fueling site to be down.
solution: Technician located the back office PC in the back room, contacted the help desk, and assisted with adding the system to Commander. Connectivity was restored and the system is now operational.

problem: New dual C18 Commander point-of-sale system installation and network integration at fuel site with four Ovation2 dispensers and ATG tank probe required.
solution: Installed two C18 registers, one Commander site controller, thermal printers, scanners, M400 pinpads, three uninterruptible power supplies, Mako 24-port switch, Mako firewall, and Edge 100 media box with all cabling. Loaded Commander and both registers with BP Base 055 software, successfully paired registers with site controller, configured network interfaces, loaded wireless access point and switch with Ovation/Mako/Verifone/BP templates (confirmed with vendor technical support), loaded pinpads with kernel 703 package and injected debit keys, and initiated trigger pull and table load with chip transaction testing. Recommend completing Ovation2 dispenser communication establishment and upgrades, verifying graphics alignment and nozzle colors, and confirming ATG tank probe and inventory management system (MTI) setup and startup.

problem: Fueling point 4 was dispensing fuel but not registering sales on the customer display; one customer was charged only $11 for a full tank.
solution: Not stated
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x WAYNE Solenoid Valve Proportio ; 1x WAYNE Check Valve and Relief, ; 1x WAYNE Vista Switch CLR Core Gr

problem: Fueling point 2 dispenser was down and not responding after a system reboot.
solution: The technician identified a problem with the jade board and returned with a replacement jade board, which was installed, reloaded with the latest IDPOS software, and configured. A test transaction and dispense cycle were performed to verify the repair.

problem: The ATG tank probe was not communicating with the system, triggering an active 'Probe Out' alarm.
solution: The technician inspected the probe connection at the above ground tank, disconnected and properly re-secured the cable to the probe, then restored power to the ATG to reset the system. The probe now communicates correctly and fuel level readings are accurate.

problem: Fueling point 8 screen is not working and the drive-through printer is not printing receipts correctly due to a corrupt ANTX board on the dispenser and previous menu configuration changes that are preventing proper kitchen printer output.
solution: Technician identified that the ANTX board on fueling point 8 is corrupted and requires replacement; technician also found that a previous technician had worked around a menu configuration issue by adding deli items to the listing, which is causing unwanted deli items to print on the kitchen printer. Technician updated the ATG email address from the old Outlook account to patelterry75@gmail.com and will return with a replacement ANTX board to restore fueling point 8 functionality; the site should coordinate with Mercury One to reconfigure menu items back to the correct departments to resolve the printer issue permanently.

problem: Dispenser pump #3 was not stopping when fueling, and nozzle would not trip off.
solution: Replaced the broken nozzle latch and replaced the clogged filter that was causing slower flow. After both replacements, dispensers 3 and 4 returned to normal operation.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400] ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70120]

problem: Fueling point 6 nozzle was broken, causing the auto shut-off function to fail and the pump to be out of service.
solution: Replaced the broken nozzle on fueling point 6 and tested for leaks. The fueling point was monitored and confirmed to be working properly with no issues.

problem: Dispenser was offline with SSL sign-on errors, unable to process debit cards, and UPS battery was beeping.
solution: Technician checked cabling and network connections, rebooted the firewall, and confirmed all connections were communicating with Mako systems. Determined the SSL error was caused by an Invenco outage on Chevron's end. Noted that the UPS battery needs replacement as the unit only has media connected to it.

problem: Fueling point 1 regular grade dispenser was delivering slow flow at less than 5 gallons per minute, with filters dirty since February and submersible turbine pump for tank one outputting only 20 PSI instead of the required 30 PSI.
solution: Technician changed all dirty filters, tested the pump pressure with a gauge, replaced the submersible turbine pump for tank one, and replaced all six regular grade nozzles. Flow rate was restored to 8 to 10 gallons per minute.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP 1-1/2 HP Pump Motor ; 6x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Tank 10 ATG probe was inoperative due to corroded connection.
solution: Technician replaced the probe and alternative float kit on the second visit. After replacement testing confirmed the probe was functioning at 100%.
parts: 1x MAG PLUS FLOAT KIT4" ALT FLUID [846400-004] ; 1x 8884                    RDM, V/R MAG+ALT FUEL PROBE 8'

problem: Fueling point 6 dispenser was displaying a closed status and not dispensing any products, including E-85 and other fuel grades.
solution: Technician verified programming and configuration were correct, then attempted to resolve the issue by reloading the iGem, upgrading firmware, and applying the appropriate template. When the issue persisted, the iGem was swapped from the out-of-service fueling point 5 and installed in fueling point 6. After updating the fueling position configuration and bringing the dispenser online, all fuel grades dispensed successfully and all tests passed.

problem: Fueling point 2 was unable to read EMV chip cards.
solution: Technician inspected the unit, confirmed it was not reading chips, replaced the unit, and tested it to verify full functionality.

problem: Fueling point 7 unihose auto shutoff was not engaging, causing spills.
solution: Replaced the defective nozzle on fueling point 7 to restore proper auto shutoff operation. The technician tested and monitored the fueling point after repair to verify there were no leaks or other issues.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 3 dispenser display had touch screen issues.
solution: Technician reloaded firmware on all fueling points' touch controller boards and recalibrated to specification PM 1410 according to the AX12 service guide, then ran and passed all test procedures. A separate service ticket was issued for a nozzle replacement needed at fueling point 3.

problem: Unleaded and premium filters on fueling points 3 and 4 needed replacement.
solution: Technician replaced two filters on fueling points 3 and 4 and purged the system. Pumps were verified to be working properly with no leaks detected.

problem: All fueling points had no power due to an emergency stop being activated.
solution: Technician reset the emergency stop to restore power to all fueling points.

problem: The submersible turbine pump sump inner lid was damaged, causing pockets of liquid to collect in the sump below the pump boots.
solution: Technician removed 2 gallons of water from the sump. The damaged inner lid should be replaced to prevent future liquid accumulation.

problem: The automatic tank gauge was not printing from the day the equipment was installed; media was blocking the screen.
solution: Ran iX reset and reloaded and reconfigured jade boards on all fueling points. Tested media and loyalty identification and confirmed both are working properly.

problem: Dispenser payment system needed software upgrade from Marathon to Fiserv, with pinpad and card reader keys requiring injection and loyalty program configuration.
solution: Technician loaded latest Fiserv base software, programmed LAN and buypass tables, injected new debit keys into pinpad and fuel dispensers, and configured Marathon Rewards and Marathon Pay loyalty programs. Resolved Marathon Rewards communication issue by adding Rewards IP to device-specific routes through Cybera network. Recommend planning future upgrades as site is running outdated R2 Red Boards with OEMV 105.46 firmware.

problem: The breakaway on the regular unleaded dispenser was damaged when a customer drove off with the nozzle and hose still attached.
solution: Replaced the damaged breakaway assembly and tested for leaks to verify the repair. The issue was resolved.

problem: The fueling point nozzle would not engage and the dispenser auto shut off within seconds when attempting to start fueling.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle and tested all functions. Dispenser operated properly after replacement.

problem: The sump sensor in Tank 2 of the submersible turbine pump was sitting in a pool of fuel, causing a fuel alarm to trigger.
solution: The technician opened Tank 2 sump, located the pooled fuel around the sensor, and moved the sensor to verify the alarm condition. The technician removed approximately 2 gallons of water from the sump and confirmed there were no active leaks. Recommend cleaning out the sump completely and monitoring for future water accumulation.

problem: Fueling points 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 9 were missing splash guards on the nozzles.
solution: The technician installed splash guards on all nine fueling points (pumps 1 through 9). All equipment is now working properly.

problem: Fueling points 1 and 2 were stopping mid-sale for Supreme and Plus grade fuel because the pump motor was not activating.
solution: The technician discovered the premium grade control wire was incorrectly connected to terminal 5 instead of terminal 4 in the junction box, preventing the 120V signal from reaching the pump relay. The wire was moved to the correct terminal 4, and the system was retested and verified to be functioning properly with filter pressure stabilized at 32-35 PSI and fuel flow at approximately 8 GPM.
Retrieved another Commander backup as per previous communication.

problem: Tank monitor (Proguage) was not properly configured for communication with the PDS Solutions box.
solution: Technician configured the Proguage for serial connection with the PDS Solutions box, coordinated with PDS to confirm programming and port settings, adjusted ports as needed, relabeled Tank 3 for Rec-90 in the PDS system, and performed tests to verify all systems were operational.

problem: Fueling point 4 price per unit board was not working, causing super and regular grade to display the same price.
solution: Technician replaced the defective price per unit board on pump 2. After replacement, the dispenser came back online and pricing displayed correctly.

problem: Fueling point 3 had a COIPA error with an unresponsive touchscreen, and fueling point 4 was not printing receipts due to printer errors.
solution: Power cycled fueling point 3 and the unit returned to normal operation. For fueling point 4, adjusted the paper feed and cleaned the paper sensor; the printer returned to normal operation, likely due to pollen contamination of the sensor.

problem: Unleaded pump 1 was leaking from the meter on the right side.
solution: Technician found that the side plate o-ring had blown out and replaced it. Tested the meter and confirmed the leak was resolved with no further signs of leakage.

problem: The 87 octane grade select buttons on fueling points 4 and 6 were malfunctioning and needed replacement.
solution: The technician replaced the faulty 87 octane grade select buttons on both dispensers, installed new 87 octane labels, and performed testing to confirm proper operation.
parts: 3x WAYNE Decal, Octane Kit (85 Th ; 3x WAYNE Vista Switch CLR Core Gr

problem: Diesel filters installed on the dispenser were not rated for diesel fuel.
solution: Replaced the incorrectly rated filters with proper diesel-rated filters at no charge under warranty.

problem: Card readers on fueling points 1, 2, 7, and 8 were not processing cards, displaying 'one moment please' before returning to the regular screen instead of prompting for payment.
solution: The technician performed a hard power cycle on the affected dispensers and allowed them to fully reboot. After initialization, test transactions were conducted on all units and confirmed successful operation; all dispensers are now fully operational.

problem: Dispenser was pumping slower than normal.
solution: Replaced the 4-month-old fuel filter and tightened the drive belt by removing one link from it to restore proper tension on the pulley. Dispenser flow rate was restored to 8 to 10 gallons per minute.

problem: Customer requested to purchase replacement filters.
solution: Technician processed the filter purchase request under warranty with no labor charges applied.

problem: The Veeder-Root tank monitoring system was disabled because the liquid sensors were beeping continuously, and water was found in the submersible turbine pump and motor sumps.
solution: Removed water from the submersible turbine pump and motor sumps, particularly from the supernatant layer. Reconnected liquid sensors that were found disconnected under the dispensers and re-enabled the Veeder-Root system. Recommend investigating the source of water intrusion in the sumps to prevent recurrence.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 8 card readers (SPMs) were not accepting new Fiserv debit keys during a software upgrade from Heartland to Fiserv payment processing, and fueling point 3 had a known breached SPM.
solution: The technician replaced the SPMs (Secure Payment Modules) at fueling points 3 and 8 with new units, tested them, and confirmed they are fully functional and accepting the Fiserv debit keys. All other systems including the register, inside pinpad, loyalty program, and mobile pay were verified as operational.

problem: Fueling point 3 dispenser displayed a KSB error indicating the submersible turbine pump keypad was breached and needed replacement.
solution: Technician replaced the submersible turbine pump keypad, injected debit encryption keys, placed the unit into operational mode, initialized DCR/EMV reader, and verified the fix with a successful test transaction.

problem: Tank monitor lost programming possibly due to power outage, causing the dispenser to not print receipts and show a high alarm/liquid alarm.
solution: Reprogrammed the tank monitor and installed paper in the dispenser. Tested the system and confirmed all functions returned to normal operation.

problem: One of the two 2 horsepower motors for regular fuel dispensing was experiencing issues and suspected to need replacement.
solution: The technician changed the filter and tested all systems; no remaining pressure issues were found. A 2 horsepower motor and capacitor were brought to the site as replacement parts, with warranty status to be determined by management.

problem: Marathon Rewards loyalty program was offline and not displaying on receipts.
solution: Technician accessed Config Manager and found the merchant ID was missing '05' and TLS was not enabled in the PCATS Loyalty configuration. Added '05' to the merchant ID, enabled TLS, and refreshed the system. Tested loyalty functions at the pinpad and dispenser to confirm Marathon Rewards is now operational.

problem: Verifone monitor screen was not displaying properly, reported as a memory issue.
solution: Not stated. Another technician resolved the issue prior to arrival due to miscommunication between the customer and store. Ticket to be billed to jobber.

problem: Fueling point 4 unihose automatic shut-off was not functioning, causing the nozzle to overflow after fill-up.
solution: Replaced the nozzle. After repair was completed, monitored the fueling point to confirm the issue was resolved.

problem: Fueling point 6 unihose drive-off incident resulted in damaged swivel breakaway combo and nozzle, leaving the pump out of service.
solution: Replaced both the swivel breakaway combo and nozzle. Tested and monitored the fueling point to confirm the issue was resolved.

problem: New fueling point installation incomplete; point-of-sale system, dispensers, and tank monitoring equipment partially installed but networking and connectivity work remains unfinished.
solution: Technician unpacked and installed Commander, two C18 registers, two pinpads, three battery UPS units, and Cybera network switch. On next visit, technician will run network cables from dispensers to electrical room, install Avalan wired switch, run serial cables for fuel-control interface and digital price signs, and test all connections. Customer must provide internet connection and arrange for specialist or contractor to drill hole in marble for cable routing.
Not stated

problem: Diesel fuel dispensers at fueling points 7, 8, 15, and 16 were dispensing slowly at approximately 6 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced the diesel filters on both dispensers. After replacement, flow rates improved to an average of 8 gallons per minute, which is within acceptable operating range.

problem: All unleaded dispensers were delivering slow fuel flow at approximately 6 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced all unleaded filters as requested and purged the system. Flow rate was restored to 9-10 gallons per minute with all functions operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 2 dispenser only pumps a few cents during prepay transactions, then sends the transaction back for a refund.
solution: Technician diagnosed a failing switch on the B side top sensor as the root cause. After verifying the dispenser was not under warranty, the technician swapped the sensor with the bottom sensor as a test and confirmed no further issues. Technician also found receipt printers were jammed or low on paper and informed the site; removed state tags from sheer valves once site provided approval to sell and documented the work.

problem: Tank monitoring system (ATG) needed to be installed to allow the oil company to retrieve fuel readings.
solution: Installed the MTI tank monitor box on site and tested all readings and equipment with My Tank Charts to verify proper operation. Technician also trained the customer on pump cash/credit operations and ringing up cash sales for discounts, and applied a decal over the diesel sign on the dispenser. Recommendation: Order full receipt 90 overlays for all four sides of both dispensers.

problem: State inspector identified damaged vapor recovery and fuel drop caps on premium and regular unleaded fueling points, non-functional ATG receipt printer, and worn OPW lids requiring replacement.
solution: Technician met with state inspector, opened all sump lids and dispenser fronts for inspection, and identified components requiring replacement: premium and regular unleaded vapor recovery fuel caps, regular unleaded fuel drop cap, and six OPW lids (three vapor recovery and three fuel drop) with new gaskets. ATG receipt printer malfunction was reported but repair action not documented.

problem: Fueling point 1 display screen was not working, and fueling point 4 printer was not working due to a paper jam.
solution: Replaced the jade board on fueling point 1, then reset and reloaded the system and tested it. Cleared the paper jam from the fueling point 4 printer.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: A customer drove away from the fueling point with the nozzle still attached to their vehicle.
solution: Not stated

Technician reset, reloaded, and configured the jade board, which restored the display. Technician also noted that the card reader had no light and is not covered under warranty; replacement was offered to the customer but declined.

problem: Fueling point 1 premium push-to-start button was not registering when pressed.
solution: Upon inspection, the grade select button was found to be working properly. The technician discovered a leaking breakaway on fueling point 1 diesel and replaced it under warranty to resolve the issue.

problem: Unleaded filters on fueling points 7-10 and diesel filters on fueling points 11-12 needed to be replaced.
solution: Technician arrived on site and replaced all the filters. System was purged and flow rate was verified at 8-10 gallons per minute using on-site filters.

problem: Fueling point 7 had a disconnected unihose at the breakaway coupling due to a drive-off incident, and the premium 93 button on fueling point 1 was not working.
solution: The technician reconnected the breakaway coupling on fueling point 7 and verified the premium 93 button on fueling point 1 was functioning properly. Both fueling points are now operational.

problem: Diesel pump 5/6 was dispensing fuel slowly despite a filter change one month prior.
solution: Replaced the diesel filter underneath the fueling point and cleaned both valves to restore normal flow. Recommended tank cleaning if the slow-flow issue returns soon.

problem: Pump 12 dispenser was displaying a KSB error and was dead on arrival.
solution: Technician installed a replacement alphanumeric controller unit, tested it with a Wright Express card to verify normal operation, and placed the dispenser back into service. The replacement part is covered under APEC warranty.

problem: Fueling point 6 was offline from the network and displaying an 'Out of Service' message due to a compromised keypad.
solution: The technician replaced the faulty keypad with a new one, re-injected the debit encryption key, performed a firmware update by reloading the Jade board, and verified the fix with a successful test transaction.

problem: Fueling point 5 was offline from the network and locked, unable to enter diagnostics mode.
solution: Technician power cycled the dispenser and determined the register was failing to sync with the system. The fueling point 5 was wiped and reloaded to resolve communication dropout issues. After the reload completed successfully, testing confirmed the unit was online with no further communication drops.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 3 displayed a KSB error, indicating a security breach in the secure personal module (SPM) keypads.
solution: The technician removed the compromised SPM keypads from both fueling points and installed new keypads, ensuring they were properly secured. The debit encryption keys were injected and the units were returned to operational mode, with successful test card transactions confirming the fix.

problem: Post-Pay Fuel Discount button that was previously working stopped functioning after a system upgrade.
solution: Technician verified with Verifone that there is no capability to create a button that applies an 8-cent per gallon discount for specific customers. The system limitation was identified and job was marked complete.

problem: Software and payment system upgrade needed: system was running on Heartland VAPS payment network and required conversion to Fiserv payment processing with updated encryption keys.
solution: Technician backed up all configuration and reports, then upgraded the C-18 register and Commander system to the latest Marathon Fiserv software.
