New Fiserv debit encryption keys were installed in the M400 indoor pinpad and all SPM outdoor keypads. The payment network was converted from Heartland to Fiserv, Marathon Rewards and MobilePay were configured, and the Marathon Helpdesk was contacted to enable EMV and update the card table. The Jade board on fueling points 3 and 4 was also reloaded due to display alignment issues. All transaction testing passed and systems are operating correctly.

problem: The submersible turbine pump sump sensor triggered a fuel alarm, indicating water contamination in the unleaded tank 1 sump.
solution: The technician removed 40 gallons of water from the sump to clear the alarm condition. Verify the alarm has been resolved by calling 1-800-997-7725, option 1 then option 2.

problem: Fueling point 1-6 was dispensing fuel slowly (penny by penny), and fueling point 2-8 was offline.
solution: The technician found that the slow-flow issue had already been resolved on site. The technician rebooted both fueling points to restore service to fueling point 2-8 and monitored several transactions to confirm the issue was resolved.

problem: Atmospheric Tank Gauge (ATG) displaying L7 PUL T3/T4 sensor out alarm due to broken wraparound sensor in the interstitial annular space.
solution: Technician removed the faulty wraparound sensor from the interstitial space and installed a bell sensor in its place. After installation, the system was tested and all monitoring functions returned to 100% operation.

problem: Fueling point 6 displayed an error message on the dispenser screen.
solution: The technician cycled power to the jade board, which resolved the error.
parts: 1x WAYNE Vista Switch CLR Core Gr

problem: Dispenser communication alarm (E1 error) and all fuel pumps lost power after a fire department inspection.
solution: Technician found that a blown fuse in the electrical room cabinet had been damaged during the fire inspection. After replacing the fuse, all pumps and tank monitor communication were restored to full working order. Technician verified fuel flow to all dispensers was normal and confirmed recent filter replacements were functioning properly.

problem: The Automated Tank Gauge (ATG) was offline and stuck on the boot-up screen.
solution: The technician power cycled the ATG unit, which brought it back to normal operation with all sensors reporting online. Fuel operations confirmed the ATG was back online.

problem: The nozzle on fueling point 3 was not shutting off when dispensing fuel.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 3 and tested it to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 printer was not feeding receipts, possibly due to a mechanical roller issue.
solution: Technician inspected the printer on fueling point 1 and tested it; the printer is now functioning normally and printing receipts.

problem: Fueling point 11 unihose nozzle hold-open latch spring was missing, causing the nozzle not to disengage and resulting in overfilling.
solution: Technician replaced the swivel and nozzle on fueling point 11, and also replaced the broken nozzle on fueling point 9. Both fueling points are now dispensing fuel at full flow. Site should inspect all dispenser hold-open latches to prevent future issues.
parts: 2x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x

problem: Six dispensers needed to be installed as part of the MUSA Dispenser Replacement Project for 2025-2026.
solution: Technician installed six new dispensers at a flat rate of $3,025 per dispenser, for a project total of $18,150.

problem: Site required software upgrade from VAPS to Fiserv payment processing system with pinpad and card reader updates.
solution: Replaced two Verifone internal batteries and one current loop on the register. Migrated site from VAPS to Fiserv successfully, reinjected SPMs (secured payment modules) with new debit key, and injected pinpad with new debit key. Configured and brought loyalty and mobile payment online, then tested all inside and outside card readers with debit card transactions to verify functionality.
parts: 1x 8906                       RDM CLGB Current Loop (RS-232) ; 1x VERIFONE Key Inject Field load [999-DEP-00171] ; 1x VERIFONE PinPad VRK Load Expe. [999-DEP-00174] ; 2x VERIFONE Battery Pack Ruby2 [BPK169-001-01-A]

problem: After installing an Edge Box, the dispenser screen is blocking and loyalty functionality is not working.
solution: Opened ticket with technical support who recommended running an iX reset with reloading the Jade board and reconfiguring it. Troubleshooting is incomplete and needs to be continued on the open ticket.

problem: Fueling point 3 screen appeared black when reported, but upon inspection the screen was working properly; however, the sealing-plug module was found to be breached.
solution: Technician confirmed the fueling point 3 display was functioning correctly. The breached sealing-plug module is not covered under the current Dover contract (In-the-Open Tank Valve agreement), so no repair was performed. Customer should contact Dover to discuss coverage options or arrange separate service for the sealing-plug module replacement.

problem: Fueling point number 1 display screen was blank.
solution: Replaced the Anthem board, reloaded firmware version 1.22.7, restored temperature and brand settings, and re-established autolink connection. All functions tested and confirmed working.
parts: 1x WYN CPU BD, GEN5 WIN10 ANTX UI [WU023682-0001]

problem: Fueling point 7 dispenser screen displayed blank.
solution: Replaced the fuse board on fueling point 7/8 and tested the equipment to confirm operation.

problem: Fueling point 6 stops on a startup screen with an unexpected error message and is out of service.
solution: The technician reloaded and reconfigured the IDPOS system on fueling point 6, then tested it to verify operation.

problem: Fueling points 14 and 15 were slowly leaking fuel from their nozzles.
solution: The technician replaced the gasoline nozzle on fueling point 14 and the diesel nozzle on fueling point 15, then tested both to confirm proper operation.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" Leaded,11B, G ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded

problem: The EMV pinpad at Register 2 was not powering on.
solution: Replaced the pinpad, applied the debit encryption key, initialized EMV, and rebooted the pinpad. EMV chip and tap-to-pay transaction tests were performed and completed successfully.

problem: The automatic tank gauge was not showing any readings on the tank monitor after a brief power loss.
solution: The technician cycled the power to the tank monitor by turning it off, waiting 30 seconds, and turning it back on. The tank monitor then restored to full functionality with all readings restored and no errors or alarms.

problem: Site survey needed for Sam's Club AX12 dispenser replacement project at 12 fueling points.
solution: Completed the DFS Dispenser Replacement Site Survey form using the Appenate mobile app for all 12 fueling points as required by the Statement of Work.
Not stated

problem: Site survey needed for AX12 fueling point replacement project at Sam's Club location.
solution: Completed survey of all fueling points on 3/23/2026. Technician should submit the DFS Dispenser Replacement Site Survey form via the Appenate mobile app using debrief codes 98, 9105, or 9005.

problem: Site survey required for AX12 dispenser replacement project at Sam's Club location.
solution: Completed survey for all 12 fueling points using the DFS Dispenser Replacement Site Survey form in the Appenate mobile app as specified in the Statement of Work.

problem: Site survey needed for Sam's Club AX12 dispenser replacement project.
solution: Technician completed the DFS Dispenser Replacement Site Survey form using the Appenate mobile app for all dispensers at the location. Project identification code 9105 was assigned for Wayne dispenser replacement project tracking.

problem: Fueling points 2, 8, and 12 are continuously rebooting and stuck on the RAM loading display, making them out of service.
solution: Not stated

problem: The MTI tank inventory box was installed but could not communicate with the Veeder-Root TLS 450+ ATG tank probe because the null modem cable was missing.
solution: The technician ordered a null modem cable and returned to install it, then verified that communication between the MTI box and ATG was restored and working properly so the system could now display fuel tank levels.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 3 flapper was reported stuck and preventing grade selection, causing the dispenser to be out of service. Additionally, retractor cables for the hoses were stretched and not holding them up properly, with fueling point 2 cable completely broken.
solution: Tested the fueling point 3 flapper multiple times and found no issue; the dispenser was operating normally when the technician arrived. The retractor cables were not addressed in the visit notes.
Not stated

problem: Fueling point 5 was offline from the network due to a locked-up dispenser.
solution: The technician reloaded the SCD and jade board, then tested all systems to confirm they were working and communicating properly with the network.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 4 remote service was down, displaying 'Please Pay Inside' error and not communicating with the forecourt management system.
solution: Replaced the in-dispenser wireless unit and successfully re-paired it to the access point to restore communication. Also corrected the Topaz system network connection by moving the Cat5 cable from the network switch to the proper Cybera router port, restoring full system communication.

problem: Keypad displayed 'Keypad Unavailable' error message and was not responding.
solution: Replaced the keypad and applied debit key encryption. Placed the dispenser into operational mode and ran a test transaction which completed successfully. The keypad replacement is covered under warranty.

problem: The hold-open latch clip on fueling point 4 was broken and would not engage.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle on the dispenser. The fueling point remains in service with no reported spills.

problem: Fueling point 9 was displaying a bootloader error that persisted after multiple site resets, and fueling points 1 and 2 were experiencing slow flow.
solution: Replaced the jade board on fueling point 9, loaded and configured it, and verified operation with testing. Replaced the filter on fueling points 1 and 2 to restore normal flow.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Fueling point 1 display screen was blank following a recent storm.
solution: Technician verified operation of the display at fueling point 1 and confirmed the screen is working properly. No issues were found during inspection, indicating the problem had resolved itself.

problem: Fueling point 1 was displaying an IX Bootloader SCD message.
solution: Technician performed diagnostics and found no problem with fueling point 1. Trip charge was waived.

problem: Fueling point 2 displayed a KSB error on the keypad.
solution: Replaced the breached PIN entry module keypad, applied debit encryption key, put the dispenser into operational mode, and performed a successful test.

problem: The dispenser hose was leaking at the swivel connection.
solution: Replaced the hose swivel and the nozzle, then tested to verify the leak was stopped and the dispenser was operating normally.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 20' Hardwall ; 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x

problem: Regular grade fuel dispenser was not dispensing and the magnetic valve flow controller was in alarm.
solution: Technician reset the controller and tested all fuel products. All dispensers returned to normal operation and 100% functionality. Further investigation into the root cause of the alarm is recommended given the station is new.

problem: Dispenser had a defective current loop and two submersible turbine pumps on fueling points 1 and 4 were breached.
solution: Technician replaced the current loop and tested the dispenser. Informed the customer that the two submersible turbine pumps on fueling points 1 and 4 require replacement and offered to replace them, but the customer declined.

problem: Fueling point 4 dispenser was not showing fuel price on the reader screen because the push-to-start button was stuck due to sticky material buildup.
solution: The technician cleaned the sticky material off the push-to-start button and tested all product select buttons to confirm they are working properly.

problem: Whip hoses on fueling points 1, 2, 7, 10, and 11 needed replacement due to fire inspection violations, and long hoses on fueling points 10 and 12 required replacement; Georgia warning signs were missing from dispensers.
solution: Replaced whip hoses on fueling points 1, 2, 7, 10, and 11, and replaced long hoses for regular fuel on fueling points 10 and 12. Warning stickers were already in place on the pumps upon technician arrival.
parts: 5x IRPCO Hose, " x 4' Hardwall S ; 2x IRPCO Hose, " x 8'6" Hardwall

problem: Dispenser had a bad battery in the Integra 100 controller, and the regular submersible turbine pump sump contained fuel and water with a possible leak; fuel was also found under dispensers 13 and 14.
solution: Replaced the bad battery in the Integra 100 controller and verified all sensors were reading correctly after replacement. Changed the email address on the Integra to the correct one. Fuel and water in the regular sump and under dispensers 13 and 14 will need to be removed by a third party, and the possible leak should be investigated.

problem: Hanging hardware, filters, and pump toppers needed to be installed as part of a dispenser installation project.
solution: Installed hanging hardware on a later date than the initial dispenser installation. This installation task was billed separately and was not included in the original scope of work for the dispenser install project.

problem: Fueling point 7 had a drive-off on the unihose and was disconnected; fueling points 11 and 12 were dispensing at slow flow, approximately half normal speed for regular grade fuel.
solution: Reconnected the fuel pump at fueling point 7 at the breakaway connection and tested it back into operation. Monitored fueling points 11 and 12 dispensing and confirmed fast flow with no issues present.

problem: Fueling point 11 with unihose was disconnected from the breakaway coupling, putting the fueling point out of service.
solution: Replaced the breakaway coupling on fueling point 11 and tested it to confirm full flow operation was restored.

problem: Right-side submersible turbine pump sump contained pockets of water below the boots, left-side submersible turbine pump piping and components were corroded, and undercounter dispenser 7/8 sump contained approximately one-quarter liquid below the boots.
solution: Technician removed 8 gallons of water from the affected sumps. Recommend inspecting piping regularly and addressing corrosion on left-side submersible turbine pump components.

problem: Motor and capacitor failure on the pump for fueling point 7, and proportional valve failure.
solution: Replaced the motor and capacitor for the pump at fueling point 2, replaced the proportional valve at fueling point 7 under warranty, and tested all pumps to confirm proper operation.
parts: 1x Capacitor 40 uF ; 10x Filter 400 HS-30 ; 1x Motor 1.5 HP

problem: Fueling point 4 was offline from the network.
solution: The technician rebooted the J board on the dispenser and tested all functions, which restored the fueling point to 100% operational status.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 9 unihose nozzles were not shutting off automatically when the tank was full, affecting regular grade fuel.
solution: Technician replaced both nozzles on fueling points 7 and 9, then tested all functions to confirm proper operation. Flow rate was verified as normal at 7–10 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling point 3 universal hose auto shutoff appeared to be malfunctioning and causing minor spills, and fueling point 8 retractor cable was snapped.
solution: Technician verified that fueling point 3 nozzle was actually functioning correctly; the previous customer had not inserted the nozzle fully into the vehicle, which was not a equipment defect. Technician repaired the retractor cable on fueling point 8. All equipment tested and operating at 100%.

problem: The receipt printer door lock on fueling point 8 was not working and appeared to be stripped.
solution: Replaced the key assembly on the printer for dispenser number 8 and left a replacement key with the dealer.

problem: Fueling point 2 had a drive-away incident with the nozzle and a faulty breakaway; fueling point 1 is not dispensing fuel after credit card approval.
solution: Reconnected the breakaway on fueling point 2 and tested it. Fueling point 1 requires a new nozzle boot; technician recommends a return visit with replacement nozzle boot.

problem: Fueling point 11 unleaded dispenser was disconnected at the breakaway coupling, taking the pump out of service.
solution: Technician reattached the dispenser at the breakaway coupling and tested the pump to confirm it was operational and working properly.

problem: Fuel barrel was not grounded and lids/seals were damaged.
solution: Technician installed a ground wire to the barrel at no charge.

problem: Fueling point 4 dispenser displayed a KSB error message due to a breached keypad, and the customer reported pairing issues between the C-18 pinpad and the Commander system.
solution: The technician replaced the breached keypad, injected the Chevron debit encryption key, returned the unit to operational mode, and tested both debit and credit transactions to verify they processed successfully. The technician also repaired the C-18 and Commander pairing issue and verified proper communication between the devices.

problem: The dispenser meter was leaking product from the top plate, with approximately half an inch of product accumulated in the underground storage tank containment area.
solution: The technician retightened all bolts on the meter top plate and verified the leak had stopped through testing. On a return visit, the submersible turbine pump sump gasket was replaced with a new 32-inch rubber gasket, and the dispenser was tested and confirmed to be functioning at full capacity and ready for inspection.

problem: Diesel dispenser was not working or was very slow to dispense fuel due to a stuck lift handle switch.
solution: Technician freed up the stuck lift handle switch and tested the diesel dispensing function. The handle is slightly sticky but is now working properly.

problem: After a power outage, card transactions were declining at both inside and outside fueling points, with outside displays showing 'Decline Pay Inside,' indicating a partial system failure. Fueling point 2 was not accepting cards due to a breached keypad, and fueling point 7 had a failed Jade board.
solution: Technician reseated Ethernet connections at the store internet source and the Mako unit, power cycled the device, and reset the manager password and EMV initialization for the pinpads, restoring transaction capability. Replaced the breached SPM keypad at fueling point 2, injected debit encryption keys, and verified successful operation with a test transaction. Fueling point 7 was identified as requiring a new Jade board, but the customer declined replacement at this time.

problem: Fueling point 13 card reader was not reading EMV chip cards.
solution: The technician tested the card reader in diagnostic mode, confirmed it was not reading chips, and replaced the card reader on fueling point 13. The replacement was tested and verified to be working.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se [W2893895-002]

problem: Multiple fueling point dispensers were printing more than one receipt per transaction.
solution: Inspected all dispenser printers and found them operating properly. The fuel attendant confirmed that when a printer becomes jammed, clearing the jam and resetting the printer causes it to print receipts from previous transactions that were queued. All fueling point printers are now functioning properly with no issues observed.

problem: Dispenser register 101 was showing a remote service down error.
solution: Technician found and replaced a bad Ethernet cable on register 101. The dispenser was tested and verified to be working with the customer.

problem: Register 1 had a black screen and was not communicating with the system.
solution: Performed a system reset through the Ruby 2 diagnostics menu, reloaded the register software, and re-paired the register to the Commander system. The register is now back online and operating properly.

problem: OPW Progauge ATG installation could not be completed because electrical infrastructure was not ready; the electrician needed to run additional conduit and install a 110-volt outlet to power the Progauge.
solution: The technician evaluated the Progauge installation location and coordinated with the electrician, who committed to completing the electrical work by end of week. The Progauge installation is planned for the following week once power is available. Recommendation: Schedule Progauge installation after electrical work is confirmed complete.

problem: Fueling point 4 unihose nozzle handle was broken, preventing the pump from shutting off and releasing the hold-open latch.
solution: Replaced the nozzle on fueling point 4 and tested to confirm proper operation. Also reconnected a disconnected breakaway on fueling point 8 and tested the station; all equipment now functions at full capacity.

problem: Fueling point 2 dispenser digital price window was displaying incorrect pricing or not showing a price at all due to a disoriented digit in the premium price display.
solution: Not stated

problem: Dispenser commander display was showing error code 19 and the system was not functioning properly.
solution: Replaced the commander SATA controller and the hard drive (customer-provided), then reloaded and configured the commander and both registers with the latest Chevron software version. All systems were tested inside and outside, and the dispenser is now running normally.

problem: Unleaded dispensers had slow fuel flow.
solution: Replaced all six unleaded filters. All dispensers returned to 100% flow rate.

problem: All fuel filters needed replacement across all grades, and the bottom pin on fueling point 8 nozzle was broken, causing the nozzle to slide out easily.
solution: Replaced all fuel filters for every grade and tested all dispensers successfully at approximately 8 gallons per minute with no leaks. Replaced the broken nozzle on fueling point 8 and confirmed proper operation with no leaks.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 2x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 13x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Fueling point 6 was offline from the network.
solution: The technician loaded and configured IDPOS on fueling point 6 and tested the equipment to restore network connectivity.

problem: All dispensers at the site were dispensing fuel slowly across all grades after the pumps shut down and were restored by reboot, with half the pumps dispensing penny by penny and the other half slightly faster.
solution: Not stated

problem: Fueling point 2 dispenser digital price window was displaying incorrect pricing or not displaying a price at all.
solution: Technician arrived on site and verified the reported error with the customer. Not stated what corrective action was taken to resolve the issue.

Technician arrived on site and replaced the premium prefilters as needed on fueling points 5-8, then purged the lines and verified flow rate averaged 9 GPM. The technician used his own filters since the site did not have any in stock, and dumped the purged fuel back into the premium tank.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were leaking fuel from under the dispensers; the aluminum tube connected to the premium proportional valve on the B side had a hole at the top.
solution: Technician identified the damaged aluminum tube as the source of the leak and photographed the damage for reference. The tube will need to be replaced on a follow-up service visit.
Installed Pro Gauge and replaced diesel motor as part of ground-up project installation at no charge.

problem: Fueling point 11 diesel hose disconnected at the breakaway when a customer pulled the hose across their truck, causing a small spill and taking the fueling point out of service.
solution: Technician inspected the fueling point 11 diesel hose and found the breakaway locking mechanism was malfunctioning and could not be reconnected. The faulty breakaway was replaced and the hose assembly was reconnected. A pump test confirmed proper operation with no leaks.

problem: Fueling points 9-12 required filter replacement, possibly due to slow fuel flow.
solution: Replaced both regular unleaded and premium filters for fueling points 9-12, secured new filters, and tested for leaks. Test was successful with dispensing at 8 gallons per minute.

problem: Dispenser register remote service is down with the Commander stuck on screen A8, potentially caused by a power outage.
solution: Technician contacted Verifone and determined the issue could involve multiple components (battery, SATA DOM, USB DOM, and memory) that would require reloading after replacement. Site declined service due to approximate upfront charge of $1200 cash collateral and chose to work with another company; technician provided the updated backup collected during their previous upgrade.
Technician found a leaky union fitting under the dispenser and retightened it. All bolts holding the shear valve top were also tightened, and the dispenser was tested with no leaks present.

problem: The submersible turbine pump ball valve was stuck in the closed position, causing the unleaded tank to be unable to supply dispensers 5-10, leaving half the site without unleaded fuel service.
solution: The technician repaired the ball valves, restoring fuel flow from the unleaded tank. The site is now back in operation with all dispensers functioning.
parts: 4x ) 2” galvanized 90 degree elbo ; 8x ) 2” galvanized nipples ; 2x 2” galvanized Unions ; 2x %OPW 21BV-0200SS

problem: Dispenser software was outdated and EMV readers were not configured.
solution: Loaded the latest BP software onto the Commander and registers, upgraded 16 fueling points with Antx and EMV readers, loaded templates, and configured Antx. All systems were tested inside and outside. Recommend completing site onboarding to fully activate the system.

problem: Three of four dispenser communication receivers (DCRs) were offline, causing sales clearing issues at the register, and the dispenser could not be accessed for diagnosis due to a seized lock mechanism.
solution: Technician attempted to gain access to the dispenser using the special lock keys but found one key stuck and seized in the lock, preventing removal and unit access. A vendor will need to order and install replacement keys and/or new locks before a return visit can proceed to diagnose and repair the offline DCRs.

problem: Site payment processing system needed to be migrated from VAPS to Fiserv.
solution: Technician switched the site from VAPS to Fiserv successfully, reloaded the Verifone register with latest Marathon Fiserv software, injected Fiserv debit keys into the pinpad and outside card readers, and verified Marathon Rewards and MobilePay functionality. Onboarding to C-Site Management could not be completed because the customer did not provide their credentials.

problem: Fueling point 7 nozzle was leaking from the tip.
solution: Replaced the leaking nozzle with a new nozzle (Part No. 11BP-0400).

problem: Fueling point 6 had a broken breakaway connector on the unihose, and fueling point 8's DW12 printer is offline due to a non-functioning cutter mechanism.
solution: Replaced the broken breakaway on fueling point 6 using part number 88EZR-7575. For fueling point 8, diagnosed that the printer cutter is not working and recommended full replacement of the cutter during the next service visit.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 9 were not reading any payment cards and were out of service.
solution: Technician ran diagnostic tests on both card readers and performed live card transactions at both fueling points. The EMV and magstripe functions tested properly with no read errors detected, and transactions processed successfully. Both fueling points were returned to service and are operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 10 nozzle flapper was sticking.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle boot on fueling point 10 and verified the flapper operated freely with no further issues.

problem: Unleaded fuel dispensers at fueling points 1, 2, 7, and 8 were pumping slowly.
solution: Technician replaced unleaded filters at fueling points 1 and 2 as requested, purged air from the system, and verified no leaks. Flow rate was below 5 gallons per minute after filter replacement; other dispensers showed normal flow of 7-8 gallons per minute. Site did not have additional filters in stock for the other fueling points.

problem: Pumps 7-12 were dispensing regular unleaded at slow flow rate.
solution: Technician identified that the fuel filters were incorrect size (3/4 inch instead of required 1 inch) and replaced all filters with correct 1 inch size. Flow rate returned to normal after replacement.

problem: Fueling point 5 had no flow due to a damaged underground signal wire shared between the relay board and the IGEM (integrated electronics module).
solution: Technician tested the relay board, traced the underground cable through multiple junctions, and identified a low voltage wire that was damaged and shorting with shared signal wires. The damaged serial wire inside the pump was noted for replacement; technician recommends replacing the underground cable and the damaged serial wire component.

problem: Fueling point 5 uni-hose had a cut in it, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: Technician replaced the damaged hose and purged it of air, then verified there were no leaks.

problem: The dispenser pump drive belt was broken, causing no fuel flow.
solution: Replaced the broken drive belt with a new one and tested the dispenser to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 card reader was displaying 'out of service' and the dispenser was out of operation due to a breached pinpad keypad.
solution: Replaced the pinpad keypad, injected debit encryption, and updated firmware through the Jade board. The dispenser was returned to operational mode and allowed approximately 10–15 minutes to initialize.

problem: The interstitial sensor lost communication with the dispenser, causing intermittent offline status.
solution: Technician tested all wiring from the atmospheric tank gauge to the sensor and found no wiring issues. The interstitial sensor was replaced, and after installation, all functions tested normal with the sensor capable of going into alarm as designed.

problem: Premium fueling point was down with no pressure; the submersible turbine pump motor was not energizing properly.
solution: Technician tested the premium line and found no pressure, then removed the submersible turbine pump motor and discovered mechanical failure (internal blade damage with scraping sounds). Replaced the motor, but found only 81-85 VAC reaching it—insufficient voltage. Inspected the breaker panel, found the premium relay had two legs supplying identical voltage causing improper power delivery, and replaced the relay. After replacement, the premium submersible turbine pump energized correctly and pressurized to 32-35 PSI.
parts: 1x RED JACKET RELAY 120V COIL PLU ; 0x FFS-FEP 2 HP Pump Motor Assemb

problem: The tank monitor (ATG) was in alarm for the regular fuel tank due to an inconclusive or failed automatic tank test, with intermittent issues escalating to consistent warnings. Tank volume readings were not matching expected values.
solution: The technician ran multiple tank tests on site; one test ran for 1 hour 45 minutes then shut down prematurely. The technician suspected the probe may be failing and recommended allowing the Closed-Loop Shift Dip (CSLD) test to run overnight to determine if the tank test would pass. If the test fails, the probe will need to be replaced and tested again.

problem: Multiple fueling points were experiencing communication errors and going offline, with the commander intermittently losing connection after reboots.
solution: Technician reseated the power supply connection to the commander, reebooted fueling point 6 to restore communication, replaced the faulty wireless Avalan unit in fueling points 3 and 4, corrected the commander version on register 102, and swapped reversed scanner and printer cables. All dispensers returned to normal operation. Recommend investigating the root cause of the commander's intermittent connectivity issue, as rebooting is only a temporary fix.

problem: Fueling point 1 unihose breakaway was damaged from a drive-off incident.
solution: Technician replaced the damaged breakaway coupling and purged the fuel line. The repair was tested to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 7 dispenser display was showing pricing incorrectly with scrambled numbers on the mid-grade fuel option.
solution: Technician corrected the mid-grade display issue and restored it to normal operation. Technician also addressed a drive-off incident at fueling point 3 while on site.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 4 on the Ovation2 dispenser were displaying E01 errors and not operational.
solution: The technician reprogrammed the iGem controllers for fueling points 3 and 4 and tested them. Both fueling points were returned to online operation.

problem: Fueling point 10 unihose hold-open latch was stuck, causing a spill.
solution: Replaced the nozzle and swivel on fueling point 12 and purged the affected line to resolve the issue.
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x

problem: The unleaded tank 1 fill cap was broken and needed replacement.
solution: The technician arrived on site and replaced the unleaded top seal fuel cap.
parts: 1x OPW Top Seal Fill Cap, 4", EVR

problem: Fueling point 11 unihose had a crack causing a leak during operation, so the dispenser was taken out of service.
solution: Replaced the cracked whip hose on fueling point 11 and tested the system to confirm no additional leaks were present.

problem: Fueling point 6 dispenser pump would not reboot.
solution: Technician cycled power to the pump and cycled power to the J board, then tested all functions which passed.

problem: Fueling point 7 displayed E01 error, fueling points 5 and 6 were stuck on Ovation splash screen, and fueling point 3 showed KSB error.
solution: Power cycled fueling points 7, 5, 6, and 8; fueling point 7 became operational. Replaced Jade boards on fueling points 5 and 6, reloaded and configured them, then tested successfully. Replaced one submersible turbine pump on fueling point 3 and tested with debit card.
parts: 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK [W2894472-002-700] ; 2x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc [WU011879-0001-R]

problem: Fueling points 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8 had black screens and KSB errors on their keypads, causing dispensers to go offline.
solution: Replaced six secured payment module (SPM) units across the affected fueling points. On fueling point 3, performed an iXReset on the Jade board and reloaded the software to resolve continuous rebooting. Power-cycled the in-dispenser 485 communication boxes on fueling points 7 and 8 to restore communication. All fueling positions were verified online and operating normally after completion.

problem: The M400 PIN pad was declining all debit transactions because an incorrect debit key encryption was initially ordered and installed.
solution: The technician verified the key serial number (KSN), identified that the wrong debit encryption key had been provisioned, and returned on 2/26/2026 to inject the correct debit key into the M400 PIN pad. After injection, debit card transactions were tested and confirmed working.
parts: 1x VERIFONE Annual S/ware Mtc. [888-ASM-70056] ; 1x VERIFONE PSP HelpDesk, Command [888-PCS-71043] ; 1x VERIFONE Commander Site Contro [M149-111-00-NAA] ; 1x VERIFONE C18 POS Workstation, [M182-001-01-NAA] ; 1x M400 PIN PAD VERIFONE SYSTEMS [M445-403-01-WWA-5] ; 1x VERIFONE Multiport Cable Assy, [MSC445-011-00-A] ; 1x VERIFONE Thermal Printer RP-33 [P040-02-030] ; 1x VERIFONE UPS, Conditioned [P040-07-050] ; 1x CASH DRAWER FOR VERIFONE [P050-01-200] ; 1x VERIFONE PS+Ferrite, 120VAC/12 [PWR445-001-01-A] ; 1x VERIFONE 367-5249-A LOW CONTO [STA000-004-01-A]

problem: The nozzle on fueling point 16 was not disengaging and continued to dispense fuel because the auto shutoff spring was missing.
solution: The technician replaced the malfunctioned nozzle and tested it successfully with no leaks.

problem: The annular interstitial space sensor on the regular fuel tank was stuck, triggering a fuel alarm on the tank monitor.
solution: The technician removed the stuck sensor, cleaned it, reinstalled it, and cleared the alarm on the tank monitor. The system returned to normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 5 dispenser was offline with a blank customer display screen.
solution: Technician rebooted the j-board and the display came back online. The dispenser is now fully operational and functioning normally.

problem: Fueling point 5 diesel dispenser was leaking fuel from one of the hoses and taken out of service.
solution: Technician identified the leak at the top of the 4-foot diesel hose on fueling point 5, replaced the defective hose, and performed operational testing to confirm the unit functions properly with no leaks. All test fuel was returned to the diesel tank.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 4' Hardwall S ; 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 4' Hardwall S

problem: Fueling point 10 card reader (EMV reader) was offline with a blank black screen; a reboot attempt did not resolve the issue.
solution: Technician performed a full power cycle of the dispenser, reseated all communication harnesses between the SCD and Jade board, replaced the Jade board, and reloaded the SCD firmware. After reconfiguring dispenser settings and allowing the unit to fully reboot, communication with the POS was re-established and a test transaction verified proper operation. Additionally, the product filter on fueling point 5 (diesel) was replaced to correct a slow-flow condition that caused a fuel spill, and flow testing confirmed proper operation at 8 GPM.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread ; 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Pump 4 had a KSB error, pump 1 grade select button was not functioning, and a nozzle was leaking at the boot on the non-ethanol fueling point.
solution: Not stated
parts: 1x OPW Nozzle, 3/4" 11B, Unleaded [11BP-0400] ; 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK [W2894472-002-700] ; 1x WAYNE Vista Switch, CLR Core, [WU000805-0006] ; 1x WAYNE Nozzle Boot Plastic PTS [WU003091-0002]

problem: Fueling point 7 unihose flapper was not engaging correctly when the handle was released from the nozzle boot, causing intermittent activation failures and push-to-start buttons not turning on.
solution: Replaced the nozzle boot assembly with a new one and tested the fueling point back to 100% functionality. Also repaired a broken retractor cable on fueling point 10 during the visit.

problem: A card was stuck in the card reader of fueling point 10, causing the fueling point to be out of service.
solution: The technician removed the stuck card from the reader, restoring the fueling point to service.

problem: All fueling point pumps were not working after a power outage; the regular grade relay was stuck in the powered-off position.
solution: Technician unstuck the stuck regular relay and tested all grades. On the second visit, the relay contactor for regular was found completely burned out with burnt terminals, so it was replaced along with diesel filters on dispensers one and two. All grades tested at 100% functionality after replacement.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Relay, 30A, 120 Volt C ; 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Fueling point 12 display was showing 'We're getting things ready' and the dispenser was out of service.
solution: Technician arrived on site and inspected fueling point 12, finding the dispenser operational and in service with no active faults or issues. Normal functionality was confirmed through monitoring, and no repairs were required.

problem: Fueling point 2 unihose was leaking during dispensing operations.
solution: Inspected the fueling point and found the leak was actually from a damaged 4-foot hose that was ripped, not from the breakaway as initially reported. Replaced the faulty hose and performed leak testing to confirm the repair was successful with no leaks observed.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 3 were stuck displaying 'Pay Inside Loading Started' and would not advance past that screen.
solution: The technician reloaded both Jade boards with the latest iXPay EMV version 2.8.215.6, reconfigured the boards, and performed system reboots. After verification with successful test transactions, both fueling points returned to normal operation.

problem: The point-of-sale system was unplugged during counter replacement and needed to be rewired and reconnected.
solution: The disconnected cables were reconnected and secured. Shell was contacted to reactivate the site, after which debit and credit transactions were tested and verified as working both inside and outside the premises. The site is now fully operational.

problem: First register stuck in boot menu and second register not pairing with the system; commander and all registers were down.
solution: Upgraded commander and both registers from firmware 1.16.50 to 1.17.05 and reconfigured them. Replaced one battery and one SATA DOM on register 101. All systems tested after table download and verified running properly with Chevron loyalty and P97 configured and online.
parts: 1x VERIFONE Battery Pack Ruby2 [BPK169-001-01-A] ; 1x Verifone Sata Dom 2Gb OS 3.11. [MSC169-005-01-E]

problem: Pinpad was not synchronizing with the XPI application, causing a point-of-sale digital error on register 101.
solution: Reloaded the pinpad, initialized the EMV reader, and tested both EMV and tap-to-pay functionality. Both payment methods tested successfully with no further issues.

problem: All fueling points would not shut off during early morning cold weather conditions, though the issue resolved when the temperature warmed up.
solution: Technician arrived to investigate reported slow flow issues on all fueling points but was unable to reproduce the problem during the service visit. Pressure remained stable and flow dispensed normally. Site was monitored with no issues found. If slow flow or shut-off problems return, technician will return to troubleshoot the submersible turbine pump.

problem: Deli prices were displaying incorrectly and receipts were not printing because Mercuryone made changes to the product lookup units (PLUs) and menu keys without backing up the original configuration, resulting in missing deli items and wrong prices.
solution: The technician restored the missing deli items (Ham, Red Hot, Smoked Sausage, and Spicy Chicken) to the menu, corrected the fountain drink prices and menu setup, and synchronized all prices with the customer's requirements. The technician also provided training on how to prevent this issue in the future by maintaining configuration backups before making changes.

problem: Fueling point 1 had a slow fuel leak from the nozzle, fueling points 14 and 16 were stuck in a reboot loop, and the card reader on fueling point 16 was dirty.
solution: Technician replaced the bad breakaway on the dispenser nozzle and tested it to full function. Contacted Kent Hamel to unhalt fueling points 1, 2, and 14. Cleaned the dirty card reader on fueling point 16.

problem: Tank monitor was in alarm and non-responsive after power loss and reset due to a bad CPU board, and subsequently false alarm readings on the diesel tank probe.
solution: Replaced the defective tank monitor CPU board with a new nano tank monitoring system. Discovered and replaced a broken wire between the tank probes (approximately 6 feet) and installed two new seal packs, then reprogrammed the entire tank monitor system. On a follow-up visit, removed and cleaned the diesel tank probe, then reinstalled it, which resolved the false alarm readings.
parts: 3x 3M Scotchcast Connector Sealin ; 1x OPW-FMS NANO Console

problem: Fueling point 2 dispenser would not reboot and displayed an error on screen.
solution: The technician loaded IDPOs (interactive display point of sale software) on fueling point 2 and tested the dispenser to verify the reboot issue was resolved.

problem: Dispenser system was down and required software upgrade; after reload, one dispenser would not come back online after data center router restart.
solution: Technician reloaded register and commander software per manual, restored backup after pairing, corrected backup issues, programmed and tested payment system and loyalty scanner—all came online successfully. However, one dispenser remained offline; technician was unable to locate the Internet Protocol box to fully resolve the issue. Site needs to approve replacement of four to five submersible turbine pumps for the Ovation 2 system and further diagnosis of fueling point 5 (possibly requiring part replacement or maintenance).

problem: The automated tank gauge is not recording any sales from the dispensers to the TLS 450 system.
solution: Not stated

problem: Two fueling points were out of service: fueling point 11 had a card reader that was not accepting cards, and fueling point 7 had an automatic shutoff that was not working properly. Additionally, fueling point 2 had a blank card reader display.
solution: The technician tested fueling point 11 and found no issue with the card reader or submersible turbine pump. The technician checked fueling point 7's nozzle and found no issue; the customer confirmed the problem was not with the dispenser. For fueling point 2, the technician power-cycled the card reader display (powered off then back on), which restored it to full operation. All three fueling points returned to 100% functionality.

problem: The display screen on fueling point 11 was blank and not showing any output.
solution: The technician verified power was present, performed a full power cycle on the dispenser to allow complete discharge, then restored power and confirmed the display booted up normally and operated properly. No further issues were found.

problem: The dispenser hose was leaking due to wear and tear with braided metal exposed.
solution: Replaced the 8-foot hose and ran a pump test to verify the leak was resolved with no further leaks detected.

problem: Fueling point 5 unihose was leaking at the nozzle swivel, with the 4 foot hose showing damage to the braided wire covering.
solution: Technician replaced the defective swivel and the damaged hose, then performed leak testing to confirm the repair was successful with no leaks present.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 4' Hardwall S ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x

problem: The Gilabarco simulator dispenser failed testing.
solution: The technician replaced the faulty Omnia interface board and reset the programming. The unit was tested and verified to be working properly.

problem: Fueling point 13 pump hose was leaking a small amount of fuel after customers finished pumping gas.
solution: The technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 13 and tested it to verify the leak was resolved.

problem: Fueling point 10 was offline from the network and stuck waiting for the SCD (supervisor control device).
solution: The technician checked all cables for damage, replaced the SCD unit, reloaded the SCD software, rebooted the pump to allow it to sync properly, and tested the card reader to confirm all functions were operating normally.

problem: Dispenser payment system required software update and migration to new payment processor (Fiserv) with new debit keys for pinpad and card readers.
solution: Reloaded the Verifone register with latest Marathon Fiserv software, injected the pinpad and eight fuel-point card readers with new Fiserv debit keys, migrated payment processing through Cybera to Fiserv, configured the system for network and mobile payment, registered the site with Verifone, and verified all systems with successful test transactions. Customer to complete C-Store management onboarding when their account is created.

problem: Fueling point 8 unihose was not dispensing fuel after customer authorization even though the dispenser showed begin fueling.
solution: Technician found that fueling point 7 and 8 were closed off and fueling point 7 was locked on the register. Technician tested fueling point 8 in standalone mode and confirmed all fuel grades were functional, then rebooted fueling point 7, which returned the system to normal operation. Fueling point 8 was placed back in service and tested successfully with a card.

problem: All fueling point card readers were intermittently not accepting cards and displaying stuck 'Remove Card' screens, with slow card reading performance.
solution: Technician discovered that customers were removing cards prematurely, causing the system to lock up. After consulting with Dover, the issue was determined to be software-related (for which Sam's Club is responsible). Technician reset all dispensers and monitored them for 45 minutes with no recurrence. Recommendation: If fueling points #4 and #2 card readers fail again, replace them with new units.

problem: Fueling point 10 was intermittently not allowing grade selection and was out of service.
solution: Technician arrived on site and monitored the fueling point but was unable to duplicate the issue. No corrective action was performed.

problem: Fueling point 12 diesel dispenser was out of service after a drive-off incident damaged the breakaway connection.
solution: Replaced the damaged breakaway coupling and purged the fuel line to verify there were no leaks in the system.

problem: The ATG (automatic tank gauge) was offline and not communicating.
solution: The technician cycled power to the ATG to restore communication. After power cycling, the technician called in to verify that the tank gauge monitor was back online.

problem: Magnetic volumetric flow controller (VFC) was down and unresponsive after multiple power outages at the site.
solution: Technician checked the breaker and found it had tripped due to power surges; reset the breaker to restore power and verified the magnetic VFC responded correctly to multiple power load cycles. Recommend installing surge protection or evaluating site electrical infrastructure to prevent future breaker trips.

problem: Unleaded pumps at fueling points 11 and 12 were dispensing at slow flow below 6 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician arrived and found the tank was low on fuel; a fuel delivery truck had just filled the tank before arrival, which restored normal flow to 8 to 10 gallons per minute. The technician then replaced all regular filters on all dispensers and verified flow rates returned to 8 to 10 gallons per minute.

problem: Fueling points 11, 12, and 13 were not printing receipts due to paper jams.
solution: The technician cleared all paper jams from each fueling point and tested them multiple times to confirm proper operation with no errors.

problem: Fueling points 1-4 needed premium filter replacements, and fueling point 10 was unable to read Sam's club cards due to payment errors.
solution: Replaced premium filters on fueling points 1-4 and verified flow rate at 9-10 gallons per minute. Wiped and reloaded fueling point 10, testing confirmed all functions normal and card reading restored.

problem: The pinpad was stuck in a 'Waiting for Cashier' state and declining all credit card transactions, particularly EMV transactions.
solution: The technician reloaded the pinpad, coordinated with Verifone to reset and reboot the entire system, worked with Citgo to replace the trigger pull, and consulted PDI regarding control scan issues. The technician was advised to wipe and reload the pinpad to base 55 version 55.02.08, but was unable to complete this at the time of the visit; this should be completed on a follow-up visit.

problem: Fueling point 3/4 regular grade dispenser had slow flow after recent filter changes at the site.
solution: The technician changed all the filters on the affected fueling points; fueling point 3/4 flow rate returned to 100% normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 receipt printer door lock will not turn or lock.
solution: Replaced the lock assembly and tested all functions to confirm full operation.

problem: Fueling point 9 retractor cable was broken and separated from the hose, causing the hose to hang low.
solution: Technician found that the nut and bolt holding the retractor cable together were missing. Technician installed new nut and bolt and reinstalled the cable. Fueling point 9 is now fully operational.

problem: Fueling points 6 and 7 were not dispensing fuel due to failed pump processor unit (PPU) main boards and a burned communication cable on fueling point 7.
solution: Replaced the fried PPU main board and communication cable on fueling point 7, then systematically diagnosed fueling point 6 by replacing the PPU main board and all PPU display boards until identifying that the premium price display was defective; replaced it with a new unit. Technician also power cycled the 485 in-dispenser module to restore offline dispensers 3 and 4 to the commander network. All units tested normal after repairs.
parts: 1x 8346                      RDM Display Board, Programmed ; 1x WAYNE Display, Module UPD 5 x [WM040824-0011] ; 1x 3FT CAT6 ETHERNET CABLE [WU021320-0006]

problem: Display screen showed stuck error after Edge box installation, and loyalty ID prompt was hidden behind the media screen making it not visible to customers.
solution: Installed Edge 100 box with latest DXmounter and DXPromo package, reconfigured 15 fueling points jade board to enable media isense, and corrected DXPromo configuration on all 16 fueling points. All systems were tested and confirmed with DFS global support team. Additionally, replaced one swivel on fueling point 9 and power-cycled register 103 to restore cash drawer responsiveness.

problem: The dispenser motor spins continuously before the handle is lifted and the dispenser will not reset when powered on.
solution: The technician found the electronic reset module was not functioning properly. After removing and installing a new electronic reset module, testing revealed the replacement module itself had defects. The technician returned with another new electronic reset module, installed it, tested the system, and confirmed normal operation.

problem: Fueling point 1 displayed a KSB error due to a breached keypad, and fueling point 4 had a black screen caused by an unresponsive Jade board.
solution: For fueling point 1, replaced the breached keypad, injected debit key encryption, and placed the dispenser in operational mode with successful testing. For fueling point 4, replaced the unresponsive Jade board and loaded the latest iXPay version 2.8.212.4 EMV software. However, the fueling point 4 display configuration could not be completed as the unit would not accept the download; further troubleshooting may be required to resolve the communication issue with the Wayne Connect unit.

problem: All fueling points on the network were down with no lights on the access unit.
solution: Technician found the power supply cable to the Avalon unit had come loose from its socket and reseated it, which restored power and brought all fueling points except FP 1 back online. Technician noted that rebooting the commander is only a temporary fix as it goes offline again after a few days, suggesting a software or hardware issue with the commander that requires further diagnosis; however, the site declined additional service at this time. An up-to-date backup of the commander was collected.

problem: The breakaway on fueling point 8 needed to be replaced.
solution: The technician replaced the breakaway on fueling point 8 and waited for site personnel to test it to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 5 hold-open latch was sticking, causing the fueling point to be out of service.
solution: Technician changed the nozzle on fueling point 5. Fueling point 5 was restored to service with full flow operation.

problem: Fueling point 15 card reader was accepting customer cards but not releasing them and not allowing fuel dispensing.
solution: Technician arrived on site to troubleshoot the card reader issue on fueling point 15 but was unable to reproduce the problem during monitoring. No errors were found, and the call was completed.

problem: Fueling point 16 card reader and business hours were not displaying on the system and the pump would not operate.
solution: Technician arrived on site and opened fueling point 16 to troubleshoot the card reader issue. After monitoring the system, no errors were found and the issue could not be duplicated. The call was completed with the system operating normally.

problem: Fueling point 13 dispenser nozzle was leaking small amounts of fuel after customers stopped pumping.
solution: Technician tightened the nozzle and breakaway coupling, reapplied sealant to the nozzle, and retightened it to stop the leak.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 4 dispensers were giving a dispense error and would not dispense fuel.
solution: Technician removed the bags and tested each side of both fueling points. No issues were found during troubleshooting and monitoring, and the dispensers operated without errors. The site was monitored for continued operation.

problem: Dispensers have no power and are not connected to the access point.
solution: Not stated. The technician documented that the dispensers lack power and cable connections to the access point but did not state what corrective actions were taken to resolve these issues.

problem: Dispenser was not pumping diesel because the underground storage tank was empty.
solution: Confirmed that the diesel supply had been depleted. Customer was advised to order diesel fuel to restore dispenser operation.

problem: Fueling points 5, 6, 7, and 8 were not reading credit or debit cards.
solution: Technician diagnosed that the SPM keypads were breached and replaced them with new units. After replacement, technician injected debit encryption, placed the dispensers into operational mode, loaded EMV and DCR files from the point-of-sale system, and performed successful test transactions on all affected fueling points to confirm full operability.

problem: Fueling points 2 and 4 were offline from the network.
solution: Technician arrived on site and found fueling point 4 offline to the host. A wipe and reload of the software was attempted without success, then the dispenser was connected directly to ethernet. Recommendation: Verify the latest software version is installed by comparing to the current version available on the Wayne portal.
parts: 1x crq 450 3 volt battery ; 1x WAYNE CPU Board iX Jade Produc

problem: Fueling point 11 regular grade auto shut-off nozzle not working, causing three customer fuel spills; the kick-off spring was missing and the auto-shutoff system had completely failed.
solution: Technician removed the nozzle and found the spring for the kick-off mechanism was missing and the auto-shutoff system had failed completely. The nozzle should be replaced to restore proper automatic shut-off function when the tank is full.

problem: Fueling points 4, 5, 6, and 12 were not reading cards and were out of service with no error messages displayed.
solution: The technician found that the breaker for fueling points 5 and 6 was off, turned it back on, and verified both dispensers functioned properly. No problems were found at fueling points 4 and 12.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 9 experienced drive-offs where customers removed hoses without proper disconnection.
solution: Technician reseated and tested the breakaway couplings on both fueling points to confirm no leaks, and replaced the filter on fueling point 1/2. Trip charge was waived.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were displaying COIPA errors and not completing sales transactions.
solution: On fueling point 10, replaced the SPM (sales and payment module) and reloaded the appropriate software and keys, which resolved the error and restored debit card functionality. On fueling point 9, replaced the EPD (electronic pump display) module, after which the pump resumed normal operation and completed sales.
parts: 1x Anthem SPM 2.5 Blank ; 1x Wayne Display Board, Programme

problem: Unleaded and diesel filters needed replacement at multiple fueling points.
solution: Technician replaced unleaded filters at fueling points 5/6, 9/10, and 11/12, replaced premium filters at fueling points 5/6, 9/10, and 11/12, and replaced diesel filter at fueling point 11/12.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter, 1 -12 Thread ; 10x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread

problem: Slow fuel flow reported on unleaded fueling points 7 through 12.
solution: Technician arrived on site and checked all fueling positions, confirming they are dispensing at full flow. No parts were replaced.
Swivel was replaced on the hose/nozzle assembly. The non-warranty portion of the repair was billed separately to the customer.

problem: Diesel dispenser was not dispensing or dispensing very slowly, with issues identified as air in the system, bad leak detector, and faulty check valve; ATG probe connections also needed sealing.
solution: Technician removed air from the diesel system to restore full flow, sealed all four ATG probe connections with seal packs, identified and replaced a bad check valve in the system, and noted that the leak detector inside the diesel tank is also faulty and will require replacement once rainwater is removed from the sump.
parts: 1x RJ Check Valve & Sprin [001441835] ; 4x 3M Scotchcast Connector Sealin [3570G-N]

problem: The dispenser register display was showing a black screen.
solution: Reloaded the register software to restore the display functionality.

problem: Fueling point 4 dispenser had a completely black screen with no display output and the card reader was not functioning; the iX board was not powering up properly.
solution: Technician identified that the older iX Jade board was missing a retrofit solder connection on a jumper location. The jumper was installed and the board was rebooted, restoring normal operation. The card reader was tested and confirmed to be functioning normally.

problem: Unleaded fuel dispensing slow, flowing below 6 gallons per minute.
solution: Replaced all regular fuel filters and purged the system. Flow rates improved to 9-10 gallons per minute after service. Site had no replacement filters in stock.

problem: Fueling point 5 nozzle auto shutoff was not functioning, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle on fueling point 5 and tested all functions, confirming 100% operational status.

problem: Fueling point 1 pinpad and register 1 pinpad were both not working; the fueling point pinpad showed a KSB error indicating a breached SPM keypad, and the register pinpad was unresponsive despite having power.
solution: Replaced the breached SPM keypad at fueling point 1, injected debit encryption, loaded EMV software, and successfully tested the unit. Replaced the unresponsive MX915 pinpad at register 1, reloaded it with the latest ViperPay software, initialized EMV, and successfully tested the unit.
parts: 1x VERIFONE Pin Pad MX915 PCI 4.X [M177-409-01-R] ; 1x SPM2.5,US,PROD,EMV,BLANK [W2894472-002-700]

problem: The fuel hose had a hole in it midway along its length and was too long for the dispenser application.
solution: Replaced the hose with an 8 foot 6 inch hose that is properly sized for the dispenser. Tested the fueling point and confirmed no leaks are present.

problem: Fueling point 5 was unable to dispense Premium or Regular grade fuel even though grade select buttons illuminated, while Diesel remained operational and no error messages appeared.
solution: Technician diagnosed a faulty Price Per Unit control board by swapping it with fueling point 6 and confirming the issue followed the board. Replaced the defective PPU control board on fueling point 5 and verified through operational testing that the dispenser now properly resets, accepts grade selection, and dispenses fuel correctly.

problem: Fueling points 1, 2, 3, and 4 had dead-on-arrival ANTx boards with error after loading firmware version 1.24.12.
solution: Technician performed a factory reset on the boards, which resolved the error. Firmware was then loaded with version 1.24.5, and transaction and media functions were tested to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 8 nozzle was not shutting off automatically, causing fuel spills.
solution: Technician inspected the nozzle and found the auto shutoff spring was missing. Replaced the defective nozzle and performed operational testing to confirm proper shutoff operation and no leaks. Fueling point 8 was returned to service.

problem: All fueling points were not available for pay-at-pump transactions and stopped mid-sale; the regular-grade pump motor was running weak and had low fuel in the tank.
solution: Technician opened the dispensers, verified the dispenser control relays were operational, tested both grades, and found the regular-grade motor running weak with only 5 inches of fuel remaining in the tank. Recommend refueling the tank and monitoring motor performance.

problem: Unleaded fuel was seeping in the line under fueling points 5/6, premium fuel was weeping under fueling points 9/10, premium and unleaded dispensers at fueling points 1-6 were down, and the premium submersible turbine pump ball valve handle needed replacement.
solution: Technician arrived on site, found and repaired leaks in both fuel lines by cordoning off the affected area and sealing the lines. Replaced the ball valve top handle on the premium submersible turbine pump. Set the dispensers back online after containing the leak, though the second line required a special O-ring for successful sealing.

problem: Fueling point 7 dispenser display was blank and did not respond to reboot attempts.
solution: Technician cycled power to the unit and reseated the display connection, which restored service to the fueling point. The issue was resolved.

problem: The keypad on fueling point 12 would not allow customers to enter their PIN because the SPM (security pin module) was not communicating with the J board, causing certain number keys to not activate.
solution: The technician tested the SPM on fueling point 12 in diagnostic mode and confirmed it was functioning properly, indicating a communication issue with the J board. A new SPM will need to be installed to restore full keypad functionality.

problem: Fueling point 6 hose assembly became separated from the breakaway connector, causing the fueling point to be out of service.
solution: Technician reconnected the pump hose to the breakaway connector and tested the fueling point to confirm it was operational. While on site, technician also cleared a paper jam from the printer on fueling point 4, which is now printing normally.

problem: Unleaded fuel filters needed replacement at eight fueling points.
solution: Replaced all eight Regular Unleaded filters, cleaned strainers, and verified proper fuel flow. Performed operational testing and leak inspection on all fueling points; all units dispensed at 8 GPM with no leaks detected.

problem: The breakaway coupling on the dispenser fueling point was broken and leaking.
solution: Replaced the defective breakaway coupling with a new one and tested the fueling point to confirm proper operation.

problem: Fueling point 10 was frozen on the initializing screen and would not reboot due to a failed card reader that refused to detect cards.
solution: The technician replaced the card reader and tested the system to confirm it was operating normally.
parts: 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: Fueling point 2 meter was continuing to run after customers finished pumping, causing the pump to be out of service.
solution: Technician arrived on site and tested both regular and premium fuel grades, finding no leaks or active issues. The meter was opened for inspection and no debris or problems were found; the issue could not be recreated during testing.

problem: Fueling point 6 was displaying business hours and the site was unable to open; a reset did not resolve the issue.
solution: The technician found that the Host system was offline. They wiped and reloaded the IXJade software, which brought the unit back online and restored normal operation.

problem: Premium grade fuel dispensing was reported as slow, below 6 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician investigated all fueling points and found they were all pumping between 7 and 10 gallons per minute, which is normal operation. The site was at full capacity with all pumps in use, and flow rates were operating as expected; no repairs were needed.

problem: Fueling points 11 and 12 (regular and diesel) were reported to have slow flow rates below 5 gallons per minute.
solution: Technician arrived on site and checked the flow rate on fueling points 11, 12, 5, and 6, finding flow rates of 7 to 10 gallons per minute with no slow flow issue detected at the time of service.

problem: Fueling point 5 flapper was sticking and not recognizing nozzle removal, causing the dispenser to go out of service.
solution: Technician replaced the nozzle boot and tested all functions; dispenser returned to 100% operational status.

problem: Three fueling points displayed keypad breach errors due to compromised secure payment module (SPM) keypads, and three additional fueling points were frozen during register communication initialization.
solution: Replaced three SPM keypads on fueling points 1, 2, and 5, applied debit key encryption, and tested debit transactions successfully. For fueling points 3, 4, and 6, cycled dispenser power, reset the serial communication units, and reinitalized the EMV readers at the register to allow proper loading and communication.

problem: Fueling point 6 display was not showing any prices or prompts on the screen.
solution: The technician returned to the site with a j board and reloaded and configured the IDPOS system on fueling point 6, then tested it to verify operation.

problem: Scanner on register 101 was not reading product lookup codes, and pinpad 127 had no power, preventing prepay transactions at fueling point pump 1/2.
solution: Reloaded register 101 to fix the scanner issue and replaced the power adapter for pinpad 127. All equipment was tested and confirmed working properly.

problem: Premium fueling point dispenser is dispensing very slowly; the replacement motor installed on 01/04/2026 has failed within 30 days.
solution: Technician replaced the defective premium motor with a spare motor from the truck. The failed motor is covered under warranty through Apec, so only labor was charged and the trip charge was waived.

problem: All regular fuel dispensers were running slow.
solution: Technician found and replaced a faulty leak detector on the regular tank and also discovered and replaced a defective relay for the submersible turbine pump motor. After replacement and testing, all dispensers returned to normal operation.
parts: 1x FFS-FEP Relay, 30A, 120 Volt C [400215931] ; 1x 99 LD2200                  VAPORLESS Leak Detector

problem: Regular unleaded fuel was dispensing slowly below 6 gallons per minute.
solution: The technician installed a filter gauge to test the fuel pump and confirmed proper pressure of 32-35 PSI. The original filters had not been replaced since April 2025, so the technician replaced all six regular unleaded filters, purged air from the product lines, and verified all fueling points now dispense at 8 gallons per minute.

problem: Unleaded fuel dispensing slow, flowing below 6 gallons per minute at fueling point 11/12.
solution: Technician performed flow tests and confirmed output of 8 to 8.5 gallons per minute, which is normal. Changed the filter at fueling point 11/12, which was the source of the slow-flow issue. No further complaints reported.

problem: Unleaded and premium fuel filters on fueling points 9-12 and premium needed replacement; filters had not been changed since April 2025.
solution: Technician installed filter gauges to bypass filters and verify pump pressure (32-35 PSI, normal range) and flow rate (8 GPM). Replaced all regular unleaded and premium filters, purged air from product lines, and tested all fueling positions to confirm 8 GPM dispensing at each point. System is operating properly.

problem: Fueling point 7 was offline from the network and stuck on an iX ovation blue screen.
solution: The technician replaced the jade board, reset the system, reloaded the software, then configured and tested the fueling point to confirm it was operating properly.
parts: 1x WAYNE CPU PCB iX Jade Dual Sid

problem: Diesel pumps #1 and #2 were dispensing at slow flow rates.
solution: Not stated

problem: The rural unleaded submersible turbine pump sump contained a pocket of liquid below the boot seals.
solution: The technician removed 1 gallon of water from the sump.

problem: Fueling point 6 screen was rebooting intermittently, and fueling point 7 diesel grade select button was broken.
solution: For fueling point 6: performed an iXReset, reloaded and configured the Jade board, and allowed full startup completion with successful testing. For fueling point 7: replaced the diesel filter, replaced the broken diesel grade select button, tested dispensing at 8 gallons per minute, confirmed no leaks, and verified proper functionality with multiple test transactions.
parts: 1x CIMTEK Filter 1 1/2 -16 Thread [70065] ; 1x WAYNE Vista Switch CLR Core Gr [WU000805-0005]

problem: Fueling point 10 was offline from the network and not communicating with the site controller.
solution: The technician cleared and reset the site controller display (SCD), reloaded both the SCD and jade boards, and tested the system to confirm all equipment was working properly. The customer was advised to use the equipment and verify continued operation.

problem: Dispensers 1 and 2 had slow fuel flow due to clogged filters and dirty strainers.
solution: Cleaned the strainers and replaced filters on dispensers 1, 2, 9, and 10 (diesel). Tested all fuel grades, printers, and card readers; all systems are now working as expected.

problem: Fueling point 7 was freezing on the carwash prompt when customers selected 'No' on the touchscreen, requiring reboots that only temporarily resolved the issue.
solution: The technician observed multiple customer transactions and verified the dispenser was operating normally at the time of service, unable to reproduce the reported freeze. The issue was determined to be a temporary Anthem Open Architecture software or communication timeout at the carwash interface that cleared after the site's automatic system reset, with no hardware fault found.

problem: Fueling points 9 and 10 were displaying 'Please Pay Inside' error and not appearing in the register system, preventing customers from paying at the pump.
solution: Technician identified a faulty Wayne Connect in-dispenser communication module by swapping it with a unit from another dispenser and confirming the error followed the unit. Replaced the defective module and reconfigured it; both fueling points were restored to service and are now processing card transactions successfully.

problem: Fueling point 5 card reader not working and fueling point 6 nozzle leaking.
solution: Replaced the breached smart payment module on fueling point 5 and reloaded it. Replaced the leaking nozzle on fueling point 6. All issues resolved.

problem: Fueling point 2 card reader displaying 'card reader unavailable' message; reboot attempted but issue persisted.
solution: Not stated. Technician's notes document inspection and repair of multiple other fueling points (FP1, FP5, FP6, FP12, FP11) but do not specify what was done to resolve the FP2 card reader issue.
parts: 1x IRPCO Hose, " x 4' Hardwall S ; 1x OPW Swivel, 2-Plane, " Male x ; 1x CENTRAL Breakaway, ", Catlow ; 1x WAYNE Svc Kit, Card Reader, Se

problem: The fueling point dispenser system was offline due to a failed power strip, and fueling points 1 and 6 had persistent power and connection issues; additionally, the electrical panel was overloaded by canopy and building flood lights drawing excessive power, and the Gilbarco EMC tank monitor fuse was blown.
solution: The technician replaced the failed power strip with a battery backup unit, which restored fueling points 2-5 for prepay and card transactions. Fueling point 1 was restored with a power cycle from the breaker. Fueling point 6 continues to have intermittent connection issues requiring Gilbarco technical support to troubleshoot. The technician also replaced the blown 250V slow-blow fuse in the Gilbarco EMC tank monitor and identified that all canopy and building flood lights must be removed from the fuel electrical panel to prevent future overload on the 60-amp circuit.

problem: Fueling point 2 was not displaying fuel prices on the dispenser.
solution: The technician found a credit card locked in the card reader, which was preventing the dispenser from functioning properly. After cleaning the card reader and performing a warm start on the dispenser, it recovered fully and all prices displayed correctly.

problem: Fueling point 13 dispenser was reported to slowly leak gasoline from the handle after customers completed pumping.
solution: Technician inspected the nozzle, swivel, and hose on dispenser 13 and found no leaks or damage. Testing with multiple customers showed the dispenser was operating normally; the issue was determined to be customer error rather than equipment malfunction.

problem: Fueling points 3 and 4 were reported as not pumping fuel, but the dispenser was functioning normally with all three products pumping correctly on both sides.
solution: The technician verified that both fueling points and dispensers were operating properly with correct prices displayed and all fuel products pumping as expected. No hardware issues were found. The technician backed up the dispenser and reset it to 100% to ensure full functionality, likely resolving a software glitch or customer operation error.

problem: The pulsar submersible turbine pump sump contained pockets of water below the boot seals.
solution: Technician Bret removed approximately 3 gallons of water from the sump.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 have approximately one-quarter of a gallon of liquid in the sump below the pump boots.
solution: The technician removed 4 gallons of water from the sump.

problem: Fueling points 7 and 8 were dispensing regular fuel slowly.
solution: Replaced one filter on fueling points 7 and 8 and tested the dispensers to verify proper operation.

problem: All fuel dispensers at all grades were dispensing at very slow flow rates, one penny's worth at a time.
